---
title: "Heterogeneity of Treatment Effects: Reproducibility Script"
subtitle: "All analyses, tables, and figures for the secondary-analysis paper"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
    df-print: paged
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

## Overview

Public reproducibility script for the secondary-analysis manuscript for HTE analysis with Mexican Healthcare Workers.

The Distress Composite factor scores and the inverse-probability weights are
supplied as columns in the data file rather than refit here as grf's AU-TOC is
sensitive to last-bit changes in its inputs, so the exact values
entered into the published models are provided. The code that generated them is
retained, commented out, in the chunks below.

**To run:** `quarto render` from the folder holding `atme_hte_deid.csv` and the
two SuperLearner helpers. Reference environment: R 4.4.x, grf 2.5.0,
SuperLearner 2.0.29, ranger 0.16.0, glmnet 4.1.8, psych 2.4.6.26, readr
2.1.5 (readr version is important for reproduction) Every forest is fit
with `num.threads = 1` and all seeds are fixed, so the run is deterministic.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Setup

```{r setup}
#| echo: false
rm(list = ls(all.names = TRUE))

library(sg)
library(tmle)
library(SuperLearner)
library(glmnet)
library(xgboost)
library(ranger)
library(dplyr)
library(grf)
library(usethis)
library(devtools)
library(tidyverse)
library(quadprog)
library(kernlab)
library(CVXR)
library(cvAUC)
library(doParallel)
library(fastDummies)
library(janitor)
library(psych)
library(GPArotation)

cl <- makeCluster(detectCores() - 1)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# ---- Data load ---------------------------------------------------------------
# The file has no participant identifier, so a stable row-index key is
# synthesized. `id` is never a predictor -- it is an internal join key only.
# Row order in the CSV is the alignment guarantee; do NOT reorder before this.
mydata    <- read.csv("atme_hte_deid.csv", header = TRUE)
mydata$id <- seq_len(nrow(mydata))
full_data <- mydata

# The six supplied vectors must be read with readr (see Overview); base R's
# parser cannot represent some of these doubles.
shipped_cols <- c("distress_factor", "ipw_composite", "ipw_anxiety",
                  "ipw_depression", "ipw_stress", "ipw_wellbeing")
shipped <- readr::read_csv("atme_hte_deid.csv", col_select = all_of(shipped_cols),
                           show_col_types = FALSE)
shipped$id <- seq_len(nrow(shipped))
stopifnot(nrow(shipped) == nrow(mydata))
cat(sprintf("Loaded atme_hte_deid.csv: %d rows x %d columns\n", nrow(mydata), ncol(mydata)))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Predictor Construction

Identical to all source files (184 baseline predictors: item-level
questionnaire responses + dummy-coded demographics).

```{r predictors}
mydata$intervention_group <- ifelse(mydata$group == "intervention", 1, 0)

t1_num <- mydata %>%
  dplyr::select(starts_with("t1")) %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    -tidyselect::matches("(?:^|_)(complete|std_error|cut|clin)(?:$|_)"),
    -tidyselect::matches("(_99$|_no99$)")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-tidyselect::matches("^t1\\.(anx|dep|stress)\\.scale$")) %>%
  mutate(
    t1_training_hours = ifelse(is.na(t1_training_hours), 0, t1_training_hours),
    t1_consulta_ext   = ifelse(t1_consulta_ext == 2, 1, 0)
  )

baseline_vars <- mydata %>%
  select(state, simple_position_english, gender, marital_status, ed_level_reduced, profession)

baseline_vars <- baseline_vars %>%
  mutate(
    gender = case_when(
      is.na(gender) ~ NA_character_,
      tolower(trimws(gender)) %in% c("prefer not to say", "prefiero no decir") ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ as.character(gender)
    )
  )

baseline_vars <- fastDummies::dummy_cols(baseline_vars, select_columns = "gender",
  remove_selected_columns = TRUE, remove_first_dummy = FALSE, ignore_na = TRUE)

baseline_vars <- baseline_vars %>%
  mutate(
    simple_position_english = case_when(
      is.na(simple_position_english) | trimws(simple_position_english) == "" ~ "Others",
      TRUE ~ as.character(simple_position_english)
    ),
    simple_position_english = factor(simple_position_english)
  )

baseline_vars <- fastDummies::dummy_cols(baseline_vars,
  select_columns = "simple_position_english",
  remove_selected_columns = TRUE, remove_first_dummy = FALSE, ignore_na = TRUE)

baseline_vars <- baseline_vars %>% mutate(state = factor(state))
baseline_vars <- fastDummies::dummy_cols(baseline_vars, select_columns = "state",
  remove_selected_columns = TRUE, remove_first_dummy = FALSE, ignore_na = TRUE)

baseline_vars <- baseline_vars %>%
  mutate(
    marital_status = suppressWarnings(as.integer(marital_status)),
    marital_status = factor(marital_status, levels = 1:5,
      labels = c("Single", "Married", "Cohabiting", "Separated", "Widowed"))
  )
baseline_vars <- fastDummies::dummy_cols(baseline_vars, select_columns = "marital_status",
  remove_selected_columns = TRUE, remove_first_dummy = FALSE, ignore_na = TRUE)

baseline_vars <- baseline_vars %>%
  mutate(
    ed_level_reduced = suppressWarnings(as.integer(ed_level_reduced)),
    ed_level_reduced = factor(ed_level_reduced, levels = c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7),
      labels = c("Secondary", "Technical career", "Bachelor's degree",
                 "Graduate: specialty", "Graduate: master's", "Graduate: doctorate"))
  )
baseline_vars <- fastDummies::dummy_cols(baseline_vars, select_columns = "ed_level_reduced",
  remove_selected_columns = TRUE, remove_first_dummy = FALSE, ignore_na = TRUE)

baseline_vars <- baseline_vars %>%
  mutate(
    profession = factor(profession, levels = 1:15,
      labels = c("Medicine", "Nursing", "Optometry", "Dentistry", "Biomedical sciences",
                 "Psychology", "Health promotion", "Social work", "Chemistry",
                 "Radiology technician", "Laboratory technician", "Physical therapy",
                 "Nutrition", "Public health", "Other")),
    profession = case_when(
      as.character(profession) %in% c("Optometry", "Biomedical sciences", "Health promotion",
                                       "Radiology technician", "Laboratory technician",
                                       "Public health") ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ as.character(profession)
    ),
    profession = factor(profession, levels = c("Medicine", "Nursing", "Dentistry", "Psychology",
                                               "Social work", "Chemistry", "Physical therapy",
                                               "Nutrition", "Other"))
  )
baseline_vars <- fastDummies::dummy_cols(baseline_vars, select_columns = "profession",
  remove_selected_columns = TRUE, remove_first_dummy = FALSE, ignore_na = TRUE)

add_vars <- c("yrs_work", "household_people", "household_bathroom", "household_rooms",
              "household_internet", "household_cars", "age")
baseline_vars <- baseline_vars %>% bind_cols(mydata %>% select(any_of(add_vars)))

mydata <- bind_cols(
  mydata %>% select(id, intervention_group),
  t1_num,
  baseline_vars
)

mydata <- mydata %>%
  select(-t1_consulta_ext, -gender_female) %>%
  dplyr::select(-any_of(c(
    "t1_anxiety", "t1_depression", "t1_wellbeing", "t1_stress", "t1_maia_selfreg",
    "t1_hm.aware", "t1_hm.connect", "t1_hm.insight", "t1_hm.purpose",
    "t1_ffmq.act", "t1_ffmq.nonr", "t1_cog.emo", "t1_gq6",
    "t1_MBI_NA_emo", "t1_MBI_NA_pa", "t1_emo.ex", "t1_pers.acc",
    "t1_distress", "t1_meaning", "t1_compassion", "t1_iri_ec"
  )))

cat("Predictor columns:", ncol(mydata) - 2, "\n")

skimr::skim(mydata)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Distress Composite (EFA) and Scree/Parallel Figure

The Distress Composite (`distress_factor`) is a single-factor polychoric EFA on
the 31 follow-up \[t8\] items. The published scores are supplied in the data
file; the scree / Horn parallel-analysis figure for the supplement is built here.

```{r efa-composite}
# 31 follow-up items (auto-detect PROMIS; PSS-10 and WHO-5 constructed directly).
anx_items  <- full_data %>% select(starts_with("t8_promis_edanx")) %>% names()
dep_items  <- full_data %>% select(starts_with("t8_promis_eddep")) %>% names()
pss_items  <- paste0("t8_pss10_", 1:10)
who5_items <- paste0("t8_who5_",  1:5)
all_items  <- c(anx_items, dep_items, pss_items, who5_items)

efa_data <- full_data[, all_items]
n_obs    <- sum(complete.cases(efa_data))

poly_cor <- psych::polychoric(efa_data)

# Horn parallel analysis (seed identical to efa_t8_scales.qmd / v3 draft fig-pa).
set.seed(42)
invisible(capture.output(
  pa_res <- psych::fa.parallel(
    poly_cor$rho, n.obs = n_obs, fa = "fa", fm = "ml", plot = FALSE
  )
))

# Published scores, supplied in the data file. The code that generated them:
#
# efa_1f <- psych::fa(efa_data, nfactors = 1, n.obs = n_obs, cor = "poly", fm = "ml")
# distress_factor_raw <- data.frame(id = full_data$id,
#                                   distress_factor = efa_1f$scores[, 1])
# distress_factor_raw <- distress_factor_raw[!is.na(distress_factor_raw$distress_factor), ]
distress_factor_raw <- shipped %>%
  select(id, distress_factor) %>%
  filter(!is.na(distress_factor)) %>%
  as.data.frame()

cat("  Distress Composite supplied for", nrow(distress_factor_raw), "participants.\n")
```

```{r efa-scree-figure}
# eFigure — scree + Horn parallel analysis (single dominant eigenvalue → 1-factor).
pa_df <- tibble(
  factor    = seq_along(pa_res$fa.values),
  observed  = pa_res$fa.values,
  simulated = pa_res$fa.simr
)

p_efa_scree <- ggplot(pa_df, aes(x = factor)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = observed),  colour = "#01665e", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = observed), colour = "#01665e", size = 2.3) +
  geom_line(aes(y = simulated), colour = "grey40",  linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(y = simulated), colour = "grey40", size = 1.8, shape = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, max(pa_df$factor), by = 2)) +
  labs(x = "Factor", y = "Eigenvalue") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10)

print(p_efa_scree)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Outcomes

All five outcomes are joined once: the four 37-week component scales
plus the Distress Composite. All are excluded from the predictor set, so
`X_all` is identical across the composite and component models.

```{r outcomes}
# distress_factor_raw was built in memory in the EFA chunk above.
stopifnot(
  all(c("id", "distress_factor") %in% names(distress_factor_raw)),
  !any(duplicated(distress_factor_raw$id))
)

mydata <- mydata %>%
  left_join(
    full_data %>% select(id, t8.anx.scale, t8.dep.scale, t8.stress.scale, t8_wellbeing),
    by = "id"
  ) %>%
  left_join(distress_factor_raw %>% select(id, distress_factor), by = "id")

outcome_cols <- c("t8.anx.scale", "t8.dep.scale", "t8.stress.scale",
                  "t8_wellbeing", "distress_factor")

cat("Outcome missingness at t8:\n")
for (col in outcome_cols) {
  cat(sprintf("  %-20s  N missing = %d  (%.1f%%)\n",
              col, sum(is.na(mydata[[col]])), 100 * mean(is.na(mydata[[col]]))))
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Median Imputation for Baseline Predictors

```{r impute}
non_pred_cols <- c("id", "intervention_group", outcome_cols)
num_cols      <- names(mydata)[sapply(mydata, is.numeric)]
pred_cols     <- setdiff(num_cols, non_pred_cols)

mydata[pred_cols] <- lapply(mydata[pred_cols], function(v) {
  if (all(is.na(v))) return(v)
  v[is.na(v)] <- median(v, na.rm = TRUE)
  v
})

cat("Predictor NAs after imputation:", sum(sapply(mydata[pred_cols], anyNA)), "\n")
cat("Final predictor count:", length(pred_cols), "\n")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Predictor Matrix (Full — Built Once)

```{r predictor-matrix}
X_all   <- as.matrix(mydata %>% select(all_of(pred_cols)))
W_all   <- as.integer(mydata$intervention_group)
pid_all <- mydata$id

stopifnot(sum(is.na(X_all)) == 0)
cat("X_all:", nrow(X_all), "rows ×", ncol(X_all), "columns. No missing values.\n")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## SL Library

```{r sl-library}
#| echo: false
# Vendored SuperLearner helpers (ship with this repo; source by relative path).
source("create.Learner.grid.R")
source("SL.ranger.1.R")

screen.lasso.sub <- function(Y, X, family, subs = c(1:ncol(X)), alpha = 1, minscreen = 10,
                             nfolds = 10, nlambda = 100, obsWeights, dfmax = ncol(X) + 1, ...) {
  SuperLearner:::.SL.require('glmnet')
  if (!is.matrix(X)) X <- model.matrix(~ -1 + ., X)
  fitCV <- glmnet::cv.glmnet(x = X, y = Y, lambda = NULL, type.measure = 'default',
                             nfolds = nfolds, weights = obsWeights, dfmax = dfmax,
                             family = family$family, alpha = alpha, nlambda = nlambda, ...)
  whichVariable <- (as.numeric(coef(fitCV$glmnet.fit, s = fitCV$lambda.min))[-1] != 0)
  if (sum(whichVariable) < minscreen) {
    sumCoef       <- apply(as.matrix(fitCV$glmnet.fit$beta), 2, function(x) sum(x != 0))
    newCut        <- which.max(sumCoef >= minscreen)
    whichVariable <- (as.matrix(fitCV$glmnet.fit$beta)[, newCut] != 0)
  }
  return(whichVariable)
}

screen.ranger.sub <- function(Y, X, family, subs = c(1:ncol(X)), nVar = ncol(X),
                              ntree = 1000, mtry = 34, maxdepth = 5,
                              splitrule = ifelse(family$family == 'gaussian', 'variance', 'gini'),
                              obsWeights, ...) {
  SuperLearner:::.SL.require('ranger')
  if (family$family == "gaussian") {
    rank.rf.fit <- ranger::ranger(Y ~ ., data = X, num.trees = ntree, splitrule = splitrule,
                                  max.depth = maxdepth, mtry = ifelse(mtry < ncol(X), mtry, ),
                                  importance = "impurity_corrected", case.weights = obsWeights)
  } else {
    rank.rf.fit <- ranger::ranger(as.factor(Y) ~ ., data = X, num.trees = ntree,
                                  splitrule = splitrule, max.depth = maxdepth,
                                  mtry = ifelse(mtry < ncol(X), mtry, ),
                                  importance = "impurity_corrected", case.weights = obsWeights)
  }
  whichVariable <- as.vector(rank(-rank.rf.fit$variable.importance[1:ncol(X)]) <= nVar)
  return(whichVariable)
}

screen.rare <- function(Y, X, family, thresh = 5, ...) {
  if (family$family == 'binomial') {
    max_limit <- min(table(Y)) - thresh
    min_Y_val <- names(table(Y))[table(Y) == min(table(Y))]
    X_rare    <- X[Y == min_Y_val, ]
  } else {
    max_limit <- nrow(X) - thresh
    X_rare    <- X
  }
  max_freq <- apply(X_rare, 2, function(x) max(table(x)))
  return(max_freq <= max_limit)
}

screen.corr <- function(Y, X, obsWeights, r = 0.95, ...) {
  corr <- cov.wt(X, wt = obsWeights, cor = TRUE)$cor
  if (any(corr[upper.tri(corr, diag = FALSE)] >= r)) {
    high_corr     <- caret::findCorrelation(corr, cutoff = r, names = TRUE)
    whichVariable <- !colnames(X) %in% high_corr
  } else {
    whichVariable <- rep(TRUE, ncol(X))
  }
  return(whichVariable)
}

screen.two_part.lasso <- function(Y, X, thresh = 5, r = 0.95, family, obsWeights,
                                  minscreen = 10, dfmax = ncol(X) + 1, ...) {
  whichNotRare <- screen.rare(Y = Y, X = X, family = family, thresh = thresh, ...)
  X_notRare    <- X[, whichNotRare]
  whichCorr    <- screen.corr(Y = Y, X = X_notRare, r = r, obsWeights = obsWeights, ...)
  screen.x     <- X_notRare[, whichCorr]
  if (length(screen.x) < minscreen) minscreen <- length(screen.x)
  lassoVars    <- screen.lasso.sub(Y = Y, X = screen.x, family = family,
                                   obsWeights = obsWeights, alpha = 1,
                                   minscreen = minscreen, dfmax = dfmax, ...)
  return(colnames(X) %in% colnames(screen.x)[lassoVars])
}

screen.two_part.ranger <- function(Y, X, thresh = 5, r = 0.95, family, obsWeights,
                                   nVar = 30, ...) {
  whichNotRare <- screen.rare(Y = Y, X = X, family = family, thresh = thresh, ...)
  X_notRare    <- X[, whichNotRare]
  whichCorr    <- screen.corr(Y = Y, X = X_notRare, r = r, obsWeights = obsWeights, ...)
  screen.x     <- X_notRare[, whichCorr]
  rangerVars   <- screen.ranger.sub(Y = Y, X = screen.x, family = family,
                                    obsWeights = obsWeights, nVar = nVar, ...)
  return(colnames(X) %in% colnames(screen.x[, rangerVars]))
}

nvar1 <- 25; nvar2 <- 40; nvar3 <- 60

screen.two_part.lasso_1  <- function(...) screen.two_part.lasso(dfmax = nvar1, ...)
screen.two_part.lasso_2  <- function(...) screen.two_part.lasso(dfmax = nvar2, ...)
screen.two_part.lasso_3  <- function(...) screen.two_part.lasso(dfmax = nvar3, ...)
screen.two_part.ranger_1 <- function(...) screen.two_part.ranger(nVar = nvar1, ...)
screen.two_part.ranger_2 <- function(...) screen.two_part.ranger(nVar = nvar2, ...)
screen.two_part.ranger_3 <- function(...) screen.two_part.ranger(nVar = nvar3, ...)

set.seed(250829)
n_tunes      <- 6
n_real_Y     <- 500
tune.glmnet  <- list(alpha = c(0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9, 1))

tune.grid.ranger1 <- expand.grid(num.trees = 5000L, max.depth = 3:5L,
  min.node.size = as.integer(n_real_Y * c(0.05, 0.1, 0.15)),
  mtry = as.integer(c(max(2, sqrt(nvar1) / 2), sqrt(nvar1), min(nvar1, sqrt(nvar1) * 2))))
tune.grid.ranger2 <- expand.grid(num.trees = 5000L, max.depth = 3:5L,
  min.node.size = as.integer(n_real_Y * c(0.05, 0.1, 0.15)),
  mtry = as.integer(c(max(2, sqrt(nvar2) / 2), sqrt(nvar2), min(nvar2, sqrt(nvar2) * 2))))
tune.grid.ranger3 <- expand.grid(num.trees = 5000L, max.depth = 3:5L,
  min.node.size = as.integer(n_real_Y * c(0.05, 0.1, 0.15)),
  mtry = as.integer(c(max(2, sqrt(nvar3) / 2), sqrt(nvar3), min(nvar3, sqrt(nvar3) * 2))))

selected.tune.ranger1 <- tune.grid.ranger1[sample(nrow(tune.grid.ranger1), n_tunes / 3), ]
selected.tune.ranger2 <- tune.grid.ranger2[sample(nrow(tune.grid.ranger2), n_tunes / 3), ]
selected.tune.ranger3 <- tune.grid.ranger3[sample(nrow(tune.grid.ranger3), n_tunes / 3), ]

grid.glmnet  <- create.Learner("SL.glmnet", tune = tune.glmnet, detailed_names = TRUE)
grid.ranger1 <- create.Learner.grid('SL.ranger.1', detailed_names = TRUE,
                                    tunegrid = selected.tune.ranger1, name_prefix = 'SL.ranger')
grid.ranger2 <- create.Learner.grid('SL.ranger.1', detailed_names = TRUE,
                                    tunegrid = selected.tune.ranger2, name_prefix = 'SL.ranger')
grid.ranger3 <- create.Learner.grid('SL.ranger.1', detailed_names = TRUE,
                                    tunegrid = selected.tune.ranger3, name_prefix = 'SL.ranger')

grid.glmnet.screen  <- lapply(grid.glmnet$names, c,
                               'screen.two_part.lasso_1',
                               'screen.two_part.lasso_2',
                               'screen.two_part.lasso_3')
grid.ranger.screen1 <- lapply(grid.ranger1$names, c, 'screen.two_part.ranger_1')
grid.ranger.screen2 <- lapply(grid.ranger2$names, c, 'screen.two_part.ranger_2')
grid.ranger.screen3 <- lapply(grid.ranger3$names, c, 'screen.two_part.ranger_3')

simple_SL_library <- c(list('SL.mean'), grid.glmnet.screen, grid.ranger.screen1)
cvControl         <- SuperLearner.CV.control(V = 5L, stratifyCV = TRUE)

cat("SL library built. Learner count:", length(simple_SL_library), "\n")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## IPW Weights (per outcome)

Inverse-probability-of-attrition weights: arm-specific SuperLearner missingness
models (seed `12345` per fit). The published weights are supplied in the data
file; the code that produced them is retained below. The composite key
(`distress_factor`) drives all composite-model analyses.

```{r ipw-helper}
# compute_ipw_for_outcome <- function(outcome_col, seed = 12345) {
#   miss_ind <- as.integer(!is.na(mydata[[outcome_col]]))
#
#   int_idx <- mydata$intervention_group == 1
#   y_int   <- miss_ind[int_idx]
#   w_int   <- mydata[int_idx, pred_cols] %>% as.data.frame() %>% janitor::clean_names()
#
#   set.seed(seed)
#   m_int   <- SuperLearner(Y = y_int, X = w_int, family = binomial(),
#                           SL.library = simple_SL_library, cvControl = cvControl, verbose = FALSE)
#   p_int   <- as.numeric(m_int$SL.predict)
#   r_int   <- ifelse(y_int == 1, 1 / p_int, 0)
#   n_int   <- ifelse(y_int == 1, r_int * sum(y_int) / sum(r_int[y_int == 1]), 0)
#
#   ctrl_idx <- mydata$intervention_group == 0
#   y_ctrl   <- miss_ind[ctrl_idx]
#   w_ctrl   <- mydata[ctrl_idx, pred_cols] %>% as.data.frame() %>% janitor::clean_names()
#
#   set.seed(seed)
#   m_ctrl  <- SuperLearner(Y = y_ctrl, X = w_ctrl, family = binomial(),
#                           SL.library = simple_SL_library, cvControl = cvControl, verbose = FALSE)
#   p_ctrl  <- as.numeric(m_ctrl$SL.predict)
#   r_ctrl  <- ifelse(y_ctrl == 1, 1 / p_ctrl, 0)
#   n_ctrl  <- ifelse(y_ctrl == 1, r_ctrl * sum(y_ctrl) / sum(r_ctrl[y_ctrl == 1]), 0)
#
#   ipw <- rep(0, nrow(mydata))
#   ipw[int_idx]  <- n_int
#   ipw[ctrl_idx] <- n_ctrl
#   ipw
# }
```

```{r ipw-load}
ipw_outcome_cols <- list(
  anxiety    = "t8.anx.scale",
  depression = "t8.dep.scale",
  stress     = "t8.stress.scale",
  wellbeing  = "t8_wellbeing",
  composite  = "distress_factor"
)

ipw_weights <- list()

# Supplied weights. Each was produced by:
#   ipw_weights[[key]] <- compute_ipw_for_outcome(ipw_outcome_cols[[key]])
for (key in names(ipw_outcome_cols)) {
  ipw_weights[[key]] <- shipped[[paste0("ipw_", key)]]

  n_comp <- sum(ipw_weights[[key]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  w_comp <- ipw_weights[[key]][ipw_weights[[key]] > 0]
  cat(sprintf("  %-11s N completers with weight > 0: %d  |  weight range: [%.3f, %.3f]\n",
              key, n_comp, min(w_comp), max(w_comp)))
}

# Composite weight vector drives all composite-model analyses.
ipw_composite <- ipw_weights[["composite"]]
stopifnot(length(ipw_composite) == nrow(mydata), !anyNA(ipw_composite))
```

```{r ipw-diagnostics}
# Per-arm response-model discrimination (cross-validated AUC) and normalized-weight
# ranges for the eMethods. Recomputed from scratch (two arm-specific Super Learner
# response models, seed 12345 each).
miss_ind <- as.integer(!is.na(mydata[["distress_factor"]]))

fit_arm_diag <- function(arm_val) {
  idx <- mydata$intervention_group == arm_val
  y   <- miss_ind[idx]
  X   <- mydata[idx, pred_cols] %>% as.data.frame() %>% janitor::clean_names()
  set.seed(12345)
  m   <- SuperLearner(Y = y, X = X, family = binomial(),
                      SL.library = simple_SL_library, cvControl = cvControl,
                      verbose = FALSE)
  cv_pred <- as.numeric(m$Z %*% m$coef)          # out-of-fold ensemble predictions
  folds   <- rep(NA_integer_, length(y))
  for (v in seq_along(m$validRows)) folds[m$validRows[[v]]] <- v
  auc <- cvAUC::ci.cvAUC(predictions = cv_pred, labels = y, folds = folds)
  list(n = length(y), auc = auc$cvAUC, auc_lo = auc$ci[1], auc_hi = auc$ci[2])
}

d_int  <- fit_arm_diag(1)
d_ctrl <- fit_arm_diag(0)

wr <- function(arm_val) {
  w <- ipw_composite[mydata$intervention_group == arm_val]
  w <- w[w > 0]
  c(min = min(w), max = max(w))
}
wr_int  <- wr(1)
wr_ctrl <- wr(0)

ipw_diagnostics <- tibble::tibble(
  arm    = c("Intervention", "Control"),
  n      = c(d_int$n,      d_ctrl$n),
  auc    = c(d_int$auc,    d_ctrl$auc),
  auc_lo = c(d_int$auc_lo, d_ctrl$auc_lo),
  auc_hi = c(d_int$auc_hi, d_ctrl$auc_hi),
  w_min  = c(wr_int["min"], wr_ctrl["min"]),
  w_max  = c(wr_int["max"], wr_ctrl["max"])
)
print(ipw_diagnostics)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Variable Importance Group Map

```{r group-map}
group_map <- tibble(variable = pred_cols) %>%
  mutate(group = case_when(
    str_starts(variable, "state_")                   ~ "State (dummy)",
    str_starts(variable, "simple_position_english_") ~ "Job position (dummy)",
    str_starts(variable, "marital_status_")          ~ "Marital status (dummy)",
    str_starts(variable, "ed_level_reduced_")        ~ "Education (dummy)",
    str_starts(variable, "profession_")              ~ "Profession (dummy)",
    variable == "gender_male"                        ~ "Gender",
    variable %in% c("yrs_work", "household_people", "household_bathroom",
                    "household_rooms", "household_internet",
                    "household_cars", "age")         ~ "Demographics / SES",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_who5_")                ~ "WHO-5 items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_pss10_")               ~ "PSS-10 items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_promis_edanx")          ~ "PROMIS Anxiety items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_promis_eddep")          ~ "PROMIS Depression items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_hmi_")                 ~ "HMI items (t1)",
    str_detect(variable, "^t1_maia[1-9]$")          ~ "MAIA items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_fivefacet1_")          ~ "FFMQ Awareness items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_fivefacet2_")          ~ "FFMQ Nonreact items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_interper_")            ~ "IRI EC items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_cogn_emot")            ~ "CERQ Reappraisal items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_compassion")           ~ "Compassion items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_meaninglife")          ~ "Meaning in Life items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_gq6_")                ~ "Gratitude items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_globalass")            ~ "Character strengths / GACS-24 items (t1)",
    str_starts(variable, "t1_burnout")              ~ "MBI / Burnout items (t1)",
    variable %in% c("t1_training_socioemo",
                    "t1_training_hours")            ~ "Training / clinical history",
    TRUE                                            ~ "Other / unmapped"
  ))

unmapped <- group_map %>% filter(group == "Other / unmapped")
if (nrow(unmapped) > 0) {
  cat("Unmapped variables (", nrow(unmapped), "):\n"); print(unmapped$variable)
} else {
  cat("All", nrow(group_map), "predictor variables successfully mapped to groups.\n")
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## `run_hte()` Helper

Single helper (the composite-file superset; returns individual `vi_df`
for top-N selection).

```{r run-hte-fn}
run_hte <- function(outcome_col, label, direction,
                    ipw_vec, mydata, X_mat, W_all, pid_all,
                    p_cols, grp_map,
                    n_trees = 4000) {

  # Seeds identical to original individual analyses:
  #   fold assignment  -> set.seed(12345)
  #   per-fold forest  -> set.seed(123456 + fold), seed = 123456 + fold
  #   eval forest      -> set.seed(1), seed = 1
  #   AUTOC            -> set.seed(12345)

  Y_raw <- mydata[[outcome_col]]
  if (direction == "negate") Y_raw <- Y_raw * -1

  keep <- !is.na(Y_raw) & !is.na(ipw_vec) & ipw_vec > 0

  X   <- X_mat[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  Y   <- as.numeric(scale(Y_raw[keep]))
  W   <- W_all[keep]
  wt  <- as.numeric(ipw_vec[keep])
  pid <- pid_all[keep]
  N   <- sum(keep)

  stopifnot(
    sum(is.na(X))  == 0,
    sum(is.na(Y))  == 0,
    sum(is.na(W))  == 0,
    sum(is.na(wt)) == 0
  )

  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s\n    N = %d  (W=1: %d, W=0: %d)  predictors = %d\n",
              label, N, sum(W == 1), sum(W == 0), ncol(X)))

  # 10-fold cross-fitted CATEs
  K      <- 10
  foldID <- integer(N)
  set.seed(12345)
  for (g in 0:1) {
    idx         <- which(W == g)
    foldID[idx] <- sample(rep(1:K, length.out = length(idx)))
  }

  fold_results <- lapply(1:K, function(fold) {
    train <- which(foldID != fold)
    test  <- which(foldID == fold)
    set.seed(123456 + fold)
    cf <- causal_forest(
      X[train, , drop = FALSE], Y[train], W[train],
      sample.weights = wt[train],
      num.trees      = n_trees,
      seed           = 123456 + fold,
      num.threads    = 1L
    )
    list(row     = test,
         tau_hat = as.numeric(predict(cf, X[test, , drop = FALSE])$predictions))
  })

  tau_oof <- rep(NA_real_, N)
  for (res in fold_results) tau_oof[res$row] <- res$tau_hat
  stopifnot(sum(is.na(tau_oof)) == 0)

  # Evaluation forest
  set.seed(1)
  eval_forest <- causal_forest(X, Y, W,
                               sample.weights = wt,
                               num.trees      = n_trees,
                               seed           = 1,
                               num.threads    = 1L)

  # ATE
  ate_obj <- average_treatment_effect(eval_forest)
  ATE     <- as.numeric(ate_obj["estimate"])
  ATE_SE  <- as.numeric(ate_obj["std.err"])
  cat(sprintf("    ATE: %.4f  (SE = %.4f)\n", ATE, ATE_SE))

  # AUTOC
  set.seed(12345)
  rate_obj <- rank_average_treatment_effect(
    eval_forest,
    priorities = tau_oof,
    target     = "AUTOC",
    q          = seq(0.05, 1, length.out = 100),
    R          = 200
  )
  autoc    <- as.numeric(rate_obj$estimate)
  autoc_se <- as.numeric(rate_obj$std.err)
  ci_lo    <- autoc - 1.96 * autoc_se
  ci_hi    <- autoc + 1.96 * autoc_se
  pval     <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(autoc / autoc_se))
  cat(sprintf("    AUTOC: %.4f  SE: %.4f  95%% CI [%.4f, %.4f]  p = %.4f\n",
              autoc, autoc_se, ci_lo, ci_hi, pval))

  # Quintile ATE plot
  q_breaks <- quantile(tau_oof, probs = seq(0, 1, length.out = 6), na.rm = TRUE)
  grp_q    <- cut(tau_oof, breaks = q_breaks, labels = 1:5, include.lowest = TRUE)

  ate_q <- lapply(1:5, function(g) {
    idx  <- which(grp_q == g)
    vals <- average_treatment_effect(eval_forest, subset = idx)
    data.frame(estimate = as.numeric(vals["estimate"]),
               std.err  = as.numeric(vals["std.err"]))
  })
  df_q <- data.frame(
    do.call(rbind, ate_q),
    group = factor(1:5, labels = paste0("Q", 1:5))
  )

  p_quintile <- ggplot(df_q, aes(x = group, y = estimate)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - 1.96 * std.err,
                      ymax = estimate + 1.96 * std.err), width = 0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey50") +
    labs(x     = "CATE quintile",
         y     = "ATE  (positive = intervention benefit)",
         title = paste(label, "— ATE by CATE Quintile")) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  # Variable importance — individual level (needed for top-N selection from full model)
  vi_raw <- variable_importance(eval_forest)
  vi_df  <- data.frame(variable   = p_cols,
                       importance = as.numeric(vi_raw)) %>%
    left_join(grp_map %>% select(variable, group), by = "variable") %>%
    mutate(group = tidyr::replace_na(group, "Other / unmapped")) %>%
    arrange(desc(importance))

  vi_grouped <- vi_df %>%
    group_by(group) %>%
    summarise(
      n_items          = n(),
      total_importance = round(sum(importance), 6),
      mean_per_item    = round(mean(importance), 6),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_per_item))

  p_vi <- ggplot(vi_grouped,
                 aes(x = reorder(group, mean_per_item), y = mean_per_item)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue3") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x        = NULL,
         y        = "Mean split-frequency importance per item",
         title    = paste(label, "— Variable Importance by Scale"),
         subtitle = "Bars show mean per item; adjusts for scale size") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  list(
    label       = label,
    outcome_col = outcome_col,
    N           = N,
    N_treated   = sum(W == 1),
    N_control   = sum(W == 0),
    n_predictors = ncol(X),
    ATE         = ATE,
    ATE_SE      = ATE_SE,
    AUTOC       = autoc,
    AUTOC_SE    = autoc_se,
    CI_lo       = ci_lo,
    CI_hi       = ci_hi,
    p           = pval,
    CATE_SD     = sd(tau_oof),
    rate_obj    = rate_obj,
    p_quintile  = p_quintile,
    vi_df       = vi_df,
    vi_grouped  = vi_grouped,
    p_vi        = p_vi,
    tau_df      = data.frame(id = pid, tau = tau_oof)
  )
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part A — Composite Model (EFA Distress Composite)

## Step 1: Full Model (All Predictors)

```{r run-full}
cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting full model...\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")))
res_full <- run_hte(
  outcome_col = "distress_factor",
  label       = "EFA Composite — Full Model",
  direction   = "negate",
  ipw_vec     = ipw_composite,
  mydata      = mydata,
  X_mat       = X_all,
  W_all       = W_all,
  pid_all     = pid_all,
  p_cols      = pred_cols,
  grp_map     = group_map
)
```

## Step 2: Variable Importance Rankings and Top-N Selection

```{r vi-rankings}
vi_all <- res_full$vi_df

cat("Top 30 predictors by individual split-frequency importance:\n\n")
knitr::kable(
  vi_all %>%
    slice_head(n = 30) %>%
    mutate(rank = row_number(), importance = round(importance, 6)) %>%
    select(rank, variable, group, importance),
  caption = "Full model — top 30 predictors by individual split-frequency importance."
)
```

```{r define-top-subsets}
top10_cols <- vi_all$variable[1:10]
top20_cols <- vi_all$variable[1:20]
top30_cols <- vi_all$variable[1:30]

cat("Top-10 predictors:\n");        print(top10_cols)
cat("\nTop-20 (additional):\n");    print(top20_cols[11:20])
cat("\nTop-30 (additional):\n");    print(top30_cols[21:30])
```

## Step 3: Reduced Models (Top 10 / 20 / 30)

```{r run-reduced}
reduced_configs <- list(
  list(key = "top10", label = "EFA Composite — Top 10 Predictors",  cols = top10_cols),
  list(key = "top20", label = "EFA Composite — Top 20 Predictors",  cols = top20_cols),
  list(key = "top30", label = "EFA Composite — Top 30 Predictors",  cols = top30_cols)
)

results_reduced <- list()

for (cfg in reduced_configs) {
  cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting: %s\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), cfg$label))

  X_sub   <- as.matrix(mydata %>% select(all_of(cfg$cols)))
  grp_sub <- group_map %>% filter(variable %in% cfg$cols)

  results_reduced[[cfg$key]] <- run_hte(
    outcome_col = "distress_factor",
    label       = cfg$label,
    direction   = "negate",
    ipw_vec     = ipw_composite,
    mydata      = mydata,
    X_mat       = X_sub,
    W_all       = W_all,
    pid_all     = pid_all,
    p_cols      = cfg$cols,
    grp_map     = grp_sub
  )
}

cat("\nAll reduced composite models complete.\n")
```

## Pipeline Integrity Check

Verifies the composite results against **the values published in the
manuscript**, taken verbatim from the internal analysis file. This is a real
check, not a self-consistency check: the script recomputes every forest from
the raw data and must land on the paper's numbers. Any drift halts the render.

Matching exactly depends on the precision-path round-trips documented in the
EFA and IPW chunks. Remove them and the AU-TOC estimates move by ~0.005 — small
relative to their standard errors (~0.05), but enough to change which reduced
specification ranks highest.

```{r pipeline-check}
# Published manuscript values, verbatim. Expected to reproduce exactly.
expected <- list(
  full  = list(N = 1435L, N_treated = 680L, N_control = 755L,
               n_predictors = 184L,
               ATE = 0.3289, ATE_SE = 0.0472,
               AUTOC = 0.1551, AUTOC_SE = 0.0608, p = 0.0107),
  top10 = list(N = 1435L, N_treated = 680L, N_control = 755L,
               n_predictors = 10L,
               ATE = 0.3190, ATE_SE = 0.0483,
               AUTOC = 0.2020, AUTOC_SE = 0.0513, p = 0.0001),
  top20 = list(N = 1435L, N_treated = 680L, N_control = 755L,
               n_predictors = 20L,
               ATE = 0.3376, ATE_SE = 0.0470,
               AUTOC = 0.2064, AUTOC_SE = 0.0508, p = 0.00005),
  top30 = list(N = 1435L, N_treated = 680L, N_control = 755L,
               n_predictors = 30L,
               ATE = 0.3373, ATE_SE = 0.0468,
               AUTOC = 0.1937, AUTOC_SE = 0.0518, p = 0.0002)
)

tol_small <- 0.0002
tol_se    <- 0.001

check_model <- function(key, res, exp) {
  ok <- TRUE
  flags <- character(0)

  check <- function(label, actual, target, tol) {
    if (abs(actual - target) > tol) {
      flags <<- c(flags, sprintf("  %s: got %.4f, expected %.4f (diff %.4f)",
                                 label, actual, target, abs(actual - target)))
      ok <<- FALSE
    }
  }

  if (res$N          != exp$N)           { flags <- c(flags, sprintf("  N: got %d, expected %d", res$N, exp$N)); ok <- FALSE }
  if (res$N_treated  != exp$N_treated)   { flags <- c(flags, sprintf("  N_treated: got %d, expected %d", res$N_treated, exp$N_treated)); ok <- FALSE }
  if (res$N_control  != exp$N_control)   { flags <- c(flags, sprintf("  N_control: got %d, expected %d", res$N_control, exp$N_control)); ok <- FALSE }
  if (res$n_predictors != exp$n_predictors) { flags <- c(flags, sprintf("  n_predictors: got %d, expected %d", res$n_predictors, exp$n_predictors)); ok <- FALSE }

  check("ATE",      res$ATE,      exp$ATE,      tol_small)
  check("ATE_SE",   res$ATE_SE,   exp$ATE_SE,   tol_se)
  check("AUTOC",    res$AUTOC,    exp$AUTOC,     tol_small)
  check("AUTOC_SE", res$AUTOC_SE, exp$AUTOC_SE,  tol_se)

  if ((res$p < 0.05) != (exp$p < 0.05)) {
    flags <- c(flags, sprintf("  p-value significance changed: got %.4f, expected %.4f", res$p, exp$p))
    ok <- FALSE
  }

  tau <- res$tau_df$tau
  if (any(is.na(tau))) { flags <- c(flags, sprintf("  tau_df has %d NAs", sum(is.na(tau)))); ok <- FALSE }
  if (nrow(res$tau_df) != exp$N) { flags <- c(flags, sprintf("  tau_df nrow: got %d, expected %d", nrow(res$tau_df), exp$N)); ok <- FALSE }
  missing_ids <- setdiff(res$tau_df$id, full_data$id)
  if (length(missing_ids) > 0) { flags <- c(flags, sprintf("  %d IDs in tau_df not found in full_data", length(missing_ids))); ok <- FALSE }

  if (ok) {
    cat(sprintf("PASS  [%s]  N=%d  ATE=%.4f  AUTOC=%.4f  p=%.4f  tau range=[%.3f, %.3f]\n",
                key, res$N, res$ATE, res$AUTOC, res$p, min(tau), max(tau)))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("FAIL  [%s]\n", key)); cat(paste(flags, collapse = "\n"), "\n")
  }
  invisible(ok)
}

results_named <- list(
  full  = res_full,
  top10 = results_reduced$top10,
  top20 = results_reduced$top20,
  top30 = results_reduced$top30
)

all_pass <- TRUE
for (key in names(expected)) {
  ok <- check_model(key, results_named[[key]], expected[[key]])
  all_pass <- all_pass && ok
}

# AUTOC of the reduced models. The Top 10/20/30 AUTOCs are all ~0.20 and lie
# within one another's SEs (~0.05); their numeric rank order sits inside grf's
# Monte-Carlo noise and can vary by grf version.
autocs <- sapply(results_named, function(r) r$AUTOC)
best   <- names(which.max(autocs))
cat(sprintf("\nReduced-model AUTOCs: top10 = %.4f, top20 = %.4f, top30 = %.4f (numerically largest: %s).\n",
            autocs["top10"], autocs["top20"], autocs["top30"], best))
cat("  (Top 10/20/30 AUTOCs are within one another's SEs.)\n")

if (!all_pass) stop("Pipeline integrity check failed — review flags above before continuing.")
cat("\nAll checks passed. Proceeding with subgroup analysis.\n")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Benefit Subgroups — Top-20 Model

### Subgroup Assignment

```{r subgroup-assign}
tau_df_20 <- results_reduced$top20$tau_df

q20_cut <- quantile(tau_df_20$tau, 0.20)
q80_cut <- quantile(tau_df_20$tau, 0.80)

cat(sprintf("CATE cutpoints:  20th pct = %.4f  |  80th pct = %.4f\n", q20_cut, q80_cut))

tau_df_20 <- tau_df_20 %>%
  mutate(
    benefit_group = case_when(
      tau <  q20_cut ~ "Non-Responders",
      tau >= q80_cut ~ "High Responders",
      TRUE           ~ "Moderate Responders"
    ),
    benefit_group = factor(
      benefit_group,
      levels  = c("Non-Responders", "Moderate Responders", "High Responders"),
      ordered = TRUE
    )
  )

group_summary <- tau_df_20 %>%
  group_by(benefit_group) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), mean_CATE = round(mean(tau), 4), sd_CATE = round(sd(tau), 4),
            min_CATE = round(min(tau), 4), max_CATE = round(max(tau), 4), .groups = "drop")

knitr::kable(group_summary,
  col.names = c("Group", "N", "Mean CATE", "SD", "Min CATE", "Max CATE"))
```

### Descriptive Table + Heatmap + Profile Table

```{r subgroup-table}
top20_ordered <- c(
  "t1_promis_eddep04", "t1_promis_eddep22", "t1_promis_eddep29", "t1_promis_eddep06",
  "t1_pss10_8", "t1_pss10_5", "t1_pss10_2", "t1_pss10_6",
  "t1_globalass9", "t1_globalass19", "t1_globalass18",
  "t1_meaninglife3", "t1_meaninglife4", "t1_meaninglife1",
  "t1_burnout10", "t1_burnout11", "t1_burnout9",
  "t1_hmi_4", "t1_hmi_1",
  "t1_compassion5"
)
stopifnot(setequal(top20_ordered, top20_cols))

label_map <- tribble(
  ~variable,              ~scale,                          ~item_label,
  "t1_promis_eddep04", "PROMIS Depression",              "I felt worthless",
  "t1_promis_eddep22", "PROMIS Depression",              "I felt like a failure",
  "t1_promis_eddep29", "PROMIS Depression",              "I felt depressed",
  "t1_promis_eddep06", "PROMIS Depression",              "I felt helpless",
  "t1_pss10_8",        "PSS-10 (Self-efficacy)†",   "Things were going my way",
  "t1_pss10_5",        "PSS-10 (Helplessness)",          "Upset because things were out of my control",
  "t1_pss10_2",        "PSS-10 (Helplessness)",          "Unable to control important things in life",
  "t1_pss10_6",        "PSS-10 (Helplessness)",          "Difficulties piling up too high to overcome",
  "t1_globalass9",     "GACS-24 (Character Strengths)",  "Vitality",
  "t1_globalass19",    "GACS-24 (Character Strengths)",  "Self-regulation",
  "t1_globalass18",    "GACS-24 (Character Strengths)",  "Prudence",
  "t1_meaninglife3",   "Meaning in Life",                "Good idea of what makes life meaningful",
  "t1_meaninglife4",   "Meaning in Life",                "Have discovered a meaningful life purpose",
  "t1_meaninglife1",   "Meaning in Life",                "Understand the meaning of my life",
  "t1_burnout10",      "MBI (Burnout)",                  "I feel frustrated by my work",
  "t1_burnout11",      "MBI (Burnout)",                  "I feel I am working too hard at my job",
  "t1_burnout9",       "MBI (Burnout)",                  "I feel full of energy",
  "t1_hmi_4",          "Healthy Minds Index",            "I notice when thoughts lead to other thoughts",
  "t1_hmi_1",          "Healthy Minds Index",            "When I want to focus, it is easy for me",
  "t1_compassion5",    "Compassion (CEA)",               "I tolerate feelings that are part of others' distress"
) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = top20_ordered))

analysis_data <- full_data %>%
  select(id, all_of(top20_ordered)) %>%
  inner_join(tau_df_20 %>% select(id, benefit_group), by = "id")

stopifnot(nrow(analysis_data) == nrow(tau_df_20))

desc_long <- analysis_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(top20_ordered), names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  group_by(variable, benefit_group) %>%
  summarise(mean_val = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), sd_val = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(cell = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", mean_val, sd_val)) %>%
  select(variable, benefit_group, cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = benefit_group, values_from = cell)

desc_table <- label_map %>%
  mutate(variable = as.character(variable)) %>%
  left_join(desc_long %>% mutate(variable = as.character(variable)), by = "variable") %>%
  arrange(match(variable, top20_ordered)) %>%
  select(scale, item_label, `Non-Responders`, `Moderate Responders`, `High Responders`)

knitr::kable(desc_table,
  col.names = c("Scale", "Item", "Non-Responders", "Moderate Responders", "High Responders"))
```

```{r subgroup-heatmap}
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 9

scale_order <- c(
  "PROMIS Depression", "PSS-10 (Helplessness)", "PSS-10 (Self-efficacy)†",
  "GACS-24 (Character Strengths)", "Meaning in Life", "MBI (Burnout)",
  "Healthy Minds Index", "Compassion (CEA)"
)

heat_data <- analysis_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(top20_ordered), names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  mutate(z = (value - mean(value, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(value, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(variable, benefit_group) %>%
  summarise(z_mean = mean(z, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(label_map %>% mutate(variable = as.character(variable)), by = "variable") %>%
  mutate(
    scale = factor(scale, levels = scale_order),
    item_label = factor(item_label, levels = rev(label_map$item_label)),
    benefit_group = factor(benefit_group,
                           levels = c("Non-Responders", "Moderate Responders", "High Responders"))
  )

# ── Profile table: VI + subgroup z-scores ──────────────────────────────────
vi_20 <- results_reduced$top20$vi_df %>%
  filter(variable %in% top20_ordered) %>%
  select(variable, importance)

z_wide <- heat_data %>%
  select(variable, benefit_group, z_mean) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = benefit_group, values_from = z_mean)

label_map_merged <- label_map %>%
  mutate(variable = as.character(variable),
         scale    = if_else(str_detect(scale, "PSS-10"), "PSS-10", scale))

# Ordered by descending summed scale VI so the profile table row order matches
# the descending-importance order used in the Results text (Depression 0.24 >
# GACS 0.20 > PSS-10 0.16 > MBI 0.13 > Meaning 0.11 > HMI 0.10 > Compassion 0.08).
scale_order_tbl <- c(
  "PROMIS Depression", "GACS-24 (Character Strengths)", "PSS-10", "MBI (Burnout)",
  "Meaning in Life", "Healthy Minds Index", "Compassion (CEA)"
)

item_data_tbl <- label_map_merged %>%
  left_join(vi_20 %>% mutate(variable = as.character(variable)), by = "variable") %>%
  left_join(z_wide %>% mutate(variable = as.character(variable)), by = "variable") %>%
  arrange(match(variable, top20_ordered))

tbl_rows <- list()
for (s in scale_order_tbl) {
  items     <- item_data_tbl %>% filter(scale == s)
  n_items   <- nrow(items)
  summed_vi <- sum(items$importance, na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_lo   <- mean(items$`Non-Responders`,      na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_mo   <- mean(items$`Moderate Responders`, na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_hi   <- mean(items$`High Responders`,     na.rm = TRUE)

  tbl_rows[[length(tbl_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    Predictor            = paste0("**", s, " (", n_items, ")**"),
    VI                   = sprintf("%.2f", summed_vi),
    `Non-Responders`     = sprintf("%.2f", mean_lo),
    `Moderate Responders`= sprintf("%.2f", mean_mo),
    `High Responders`    = sprintf("%.2f", mean_hi),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE
  )
  # Item rows indented with non-breaking spaces (U+00A0) so the indent survives
  # read_csv(trim_ws = TRUE) and Word rendering (see Table 1 cat_block note).
  item_indent <- strrep(" ", 4)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(items))) {
    tbl_rows[[length(tbl_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      Predictor            = paste0(item_indent, items$item_label[i]),
      VI                   = sprintf("%.2f", items$importance[i]),
      `Non-Responders`     = sprintf("%.2f", items$`Non-Responders`[i]),
      `Moderate Responders`= sprintf("%.2f", items$`Moderate Responders`[i]),
      `High Responders`    = sprintf("%.2f", items$`High Responders`[i]),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE
    )
  }
}

profile_table_df <- do.call(rbind, tbl_rows)

cat("Profile table built:", nrow(profile_table_df), "rows.  VI sum check (should be 1.000):",
    round(sum(vi_20$importance), 3), "\n")

ggplot(heat_data, aes(x = benefit_group, y = item_label, fill = z_mean)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", z_mean)), size = 3, colour = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166ac", mid = "white", high = "#d6604d", midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1.5, 1.5), oob = scales::squish, name = "Within-variable\nz-score") +
  facet_grid(scale ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(scale = label_wrap_gen(18))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = "Baseline Predictor Profiles by Benefit Subgroup") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
        strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0, size = 8.5, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "right")
```

## Top-20 Evaluation Forest (Re-fit for Subgroup ATE)

```{r refit-top20-eval-forest}
Y_raw_20 <- mydata$distress_factor * -1
keep_20  <- !is.na(Y_raw_20) & !is.na(ipw_composite) & ipw_composite > 0

X_20   <- as.matrix(mydata %>% select(all_of(top20_cols)))[keep_20, , drop = FALSE]
Y_20   <- as.numeric(scale(Y_raw_20[keep_20]))
W_20   <- W_all[keep_20]
wt_20  <- as.numeric(ipw_composite[keep_20])
pid_20 <- pid_all[keep_20]

stopifnot(
  nrow(X_20) == results_reduced$top20$N,
  identical(pid_20, results_reduced$top20$tau_df$id)
)

cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting top-20 eval forest (seed = 1)...\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")))
set.seed(1)
eval_forest_top20 <- causal_forest(X_20, Y_20, W_20,
  sample.weights = wt_20, num.trees = 4000, seed = 1, num.threads = 1L)

ate_check <- average_treatment_effect(eval_forest_top20)
cat(sprintf("Re-fit ATE = %.4f vs. cached ATE = %.4f — diff = %.4f\n",
            ate_check["estimate"], results_reduced$top20$ATE,
            abs(ate_check["estimate"] - results_reduced$top20$ATE)))
stopifnot(abs(ate_check["estimate"] - results_reduced$top20$ATE) < 0.001)
```

## Subgroup ATE Test — Top-20 Model

```{r subgroup-ate-test}
stopifnot(identical(tau_df_20$id, pid_20))

idx_low  <- which(tau_df_20$benefit_group == "Non-Responders")
idx_mod  <- which(tau_df_20$benefit_group == "Moderate Responders")
idx_high <- which(tau_df_20$benefit_group == "High Responders")

ate_low  <- average_treatment_effect(eval_forest_top20, subset = idx_low)
ate_mod  <- average_treatment_effect(eval_forest_top20, subset = idx_mod)
ate_high <- average_treatment_effect(eval_forest_top20, subset = idx_high)

diff_est <- as.numeric(ate_high["estimate"] - ate_low["estimate"])
diff_se  <- sqrt(as.numeric(ate_high["std.err"])^2 + as.numeric(ate_low["std.err"])^2)
diff_z   <- diff_est / diff_se
diff_p   <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(diff_z))

cat(sprintf("Subgroup ATEs (top-20):  Non = %.3f (SE %.3f)  Mod = %.3f (SE %.3f)  High = %.3f (SE %.3f)\n",
            ate_low["estimate"], ate_low["std.err"],
            ate_mod["estimate"], ate_mod["std.err"],
            ate_high["estimate"], ate_high["std.err"]))
cat(sprintf("High vs. Non-responder contrast:  diff = %.3f  (SE = %.3f)  z = %.2f  p = %.4g\n",
            diff_est, diff_se, diff_z, diff_p))
```

## Severity Benchmark — Heterogeneity Beyond Baseline Distress (Top-20)

```{r severity-benchmark}
# --- Regenerate t1_composite (baseline distress) from the 31 baseline items. ---
# Mirrors efa_change_scores.qmd Part 1 exactly: sorted PROMIS items, then PSS-10
# and WHO-5; unrotated 1-factor ML EFA on the polychoric correlation with
# regression scores; t1 factor score standardized to its own mean/SD.
anx_items_t1  <- sort(names(full_data)[grepl("^t1_promis_edanx", names(full_data))])
dep_items_t1  <- sort(names(full_data)[grepl("^t1_promis_eddep", names(full_data))])
pss_items_t1  <- paste0("t1_pss10_", 1:10)
who5_items_t1 <- paste0("t1_who5_",  1:5)
all_items_t1  <- c(anx_items_t1, dep_items_t1, pss_items_t1, who5_items_t1)

t1_mat_comp        <- as.data.frame(full_data[, all_items_t1])
t1_item_means_comp <- colMeans(t1_mat_comp, na.rm = TRUE)
t1_item_sds_comp   <- apply(t1_mat_comp, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
poly_comp          <- psych::polychoric(t1_mat_comp)
n_obs_comp         <- sum(complete.cases(t1_mat_comp))

efa_comp <- psych::fa(r = poly_comp$rho, nfactors = 1, n.obs = n_obs_comp,
                      rotate = "none", fm = "ml", scores = "regression")

# t1 factor score (regression weights on baseline-standardized items).
t1_std_items <- sweep(sweep(as.matrix(t1_mat_comp), 2, t1_item_means_comp, "-"),
                      2, t1_item_sds_comp, "/")
t1_score <- as.vector(t1_std_items %*% efa_comp$weights)
t1_score[!complete.cases(t1_mat_comp)] <- NA
t1_composite_vec <- (t1_score - mean(t1_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(t1_score, na.rm = TRUE)

sev_efa <- data.frame(id = full_data$id, t1_composite = t1_composite_vec)

sev_df <- tau_df_20 %>%
  left_join(sev_efa %>% select(id, t1_composite), by = "id")
stopifnot(identical(sev_df$id, pid_20))        # row order matches the eval forest

# A few baseline values are missing -> median-impute so the priority vector is complete.
t1c   <- sev_df$t1_composite
t1c[is.na(t1c)] <- median(t1c, na.rm = TRUE)

# Shared AUTOC settings — identical to the primary call in run_hte().
autoc_rule <- function(pri) {
  set.seed(12345)
  ro <- rank_average_treatment_effect(eval_forest_top20, priorities = pri,
          target = "AUTOC", q = seq(0.05, 1, length.out = 100), R = 200)
  c(est = as.numeric(ro$estimate), se = as.numeric(ro$std.err),
    p   = 2 * pnorm(-abs(ro$estimate / ro$std.err)))
}

# Integrity guard: scoring the forest CATE here must reproduce the primary top-20 AUTOC.
a_forest <- autoc_rule(sev_df$tau)
cat(sprintf("Forest-CATE AUTOC (guard) = %.4f  vs. cached %.4f\n",
            a_forest["est"], results_reduced$top20$AUTOC))
stopifnot(abs(a_forest["est"] - results_reduced$top20$AUTOC) < 0.001)

# Severity-only rule, and the residualized rule (CATE | baseline distress removed).
a_sevonly <- autoc_rule(t1c)
a_resid   <- autoc_rule(residuals(lm(sev_df$tau ~ t1c)))

cat(sprintf("Severity-only AUTOC (baseline distress as priority)   = %.4f  (SE %.4f)  p = %.4g\n",
            a_sevonly["est"], a_sevonly["se"], a_sevonly["p"]))
cat(sprintf("Residual AUTOC (CATE | baseline distress removed)     = %.4f  (SE %.4f)  p = %.4g\n",
            a_resid["est"], a_resid["se"], a_resid["p"]))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part B — Component Models (4 Component Outcomes)

## Component Full Models

The four component full models (used for the 5-outcome ATE/AUTOC matrix
and for the per-component eval forests) are fit via `run_hte()` and held in
memory.

```{r component-config}
component_config <- list(
  list(key = "anxiety",    col = "t8.anx.scale",    label = "PROMIS Anxiety",    direction = "negate"),
  list(key = "depression", col = "t8.dep.scale",    label = "PROMIS Depression", direction = "negate"),
  list(key = "stress",     col = "t8.stress.scale", label = "PSS-10 Stress",     direction = "negate"),
  list(key = "wellbeing",  col = "t8_wellbeing",    label = "WHO-5 Wellbeing",   direction = "keep")
)
```

```{r component-full-run}
# Full component models held in memory (comp_full); consumed by the VI-extraction
# loop and the 5-outcome summary below.
comp_full <- list()
for (cfg in component_config) {
  cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting component full model: %s\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), cfg$label))
  comp_full[[cfg$key]] <- run_hte(
    outcome_col = cfg$col, label = cfg$label, direction = cfg$direction,
    ipw_vec = ipw_weights[[cfg$key]], mydata = mydata,
    X_mat = X_all, W_all = W_all, pid_all = pid_all,
    p_cols = pred_cols, grp_map = group_map
  )
}
```

## Step 1: Extract Individual VI From Each Component Full Model

Eval-only refit (deterministic given `set.seed(1) + seed = 1`) to
extract individual VI for top-N selection and to save the per-component
eval forest for subgroup ATEs. Validates the eval-only ATE against the
cached main-analysis ATE.

```{r extract-vi-fn}
extract_eval_vi <- function(outcome_col, label, direction,
                             ipw_vec, mydata, X_mat, W_all, pid_all,
                             p_cols, grp_map, n_trees = 4000) {
  Y_raw <- mydata[[outcome_col]]
  if (direction == "negate") Y_raw <- Y_raw * -1

  keep <- !is.na(Y_raw) & !is.na(ipw_vec) & ipw_vec > 0
  X   <- X_mat[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  Y   <- as.numeric(scale(Y_raw[keep]))
  W   <- W_all[keep]
  wt  <- as.numeric(ipw_vec[keep])
  pid <- pid_all[keep]
  N   <- sum(keep)

  cat(sprintf("    N = %d  (W=1: %d, W=0: %d)  predictors = %d\n",
              N, sum(W == 1), sum(W == 0), ncol(X)))

  set.seed(1)
  eval_forest <- causal_forest(X, Y, W, sample.weights = wt, num.trees = n_trees, seed = 1, num.threads = 1L)

  ate_obj <- average_treatment_effect(eval_forest)
  ATE     <- as.numeric(ate_obj["estimate"]); ATE_SE <- as.numeric(ate_obj["std.err"])

  vi_raw <- variable_importance(eval_forest)
  vi_df  <- data.frame(variable = p_cols, importance = as.numeric(vi_raw)) %>%
    left_join(grp_map %>% select(variable, group), by = "variable") %>%
    mutate(group = tidyr::replace_na(group, "Other / unmapped")) %>%
    arrange(desc(importance))

  list(label = label, outcome_col = outcome_col,
       N = N, N_treated = sum(W == 1), N_control = sum(W == 0),
       ATE = ATE, ATE_SE = ATE_SE, vi_df = vi_df, eval_forest = eval_forest, pid = pid)
}
```

```{r extract-vi-loop}
vi_results        <- list()
full_cached       <- list()
comp_eval_forests <- list()   # in-memory per-component eval forests (for subgroup ATEs)

for (cfg in component_config) {
  key <- cfg$key
  cat(sprintf("\n----- %s -----\n", cfg$label))

  full_cached[[key]] <- comp_full[[key]]   # in-memory full main-analysis model

  cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting eval forest (seed=1) to extract VI...\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")))
  tmp <- extract_eval_vi(
    outcome_col = cfg$col, label = cfg$label, direction = cfg$direction,
    ipw_vec = ipw_weights[[key]], mydata = mydata, X_mat = X_all,
    W_all = W_all, pid_all = pid_all, p_cols = pred_cols, grp_map = group_map
  )
  vi_results[[key]]        <- tmp[setdiff(names(tmp), c("eval_forest", "pid"))]
  comp_eval_forests[[key]] <- list(forest = tmp$eval_forest, pid = tmp$pid)

  r_new <- vi_results[[key]]; r_old <- full_cached[[key]]; tol <- 1e-4
  ate_diff <- abs(r_new$ATE - r_old$ATE); ate_se_diff <- abs(r_new$ATE_SE - r_old$ATE_SE)
  n_diff <- r_new$N - r_old$N

  if (ate_diff > tol || ate_se_diff > tol || n_diff != 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  WARNING: ATE / N mismatch vs cached main-analysis result\n"))
    stop(sprintf("ATE reproducibility check failed for '%s'.", key))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  PASS  ATE = %.4f (SE = %.4f)  matches cached main-analysis.\n",
                r_new$ATE, r_new$ATE_SE))
  }
}

cat("\nAll VI extractions complete and validated.\n")
```

## Step 2: Fit Top 10 / 20 / 30 Reduced Models for Each Component

```{r run-reduced-models}
all_results_comp <- list()

for (cfg in component_config) {
  key <- cfg$key
  cat(sprintf("\n==========  %s  ==========\n", cfg$label))

  vi_all_c   <- vi_results[[key]]$vi_df
  reduced_configs_c <- list(
    list(n = 10, cols = vi_all_c$variable[1:10]),
    list(n = 20, cols = vi_all_c$variable[1:20]),
    list(n = 30, cols = vi_all_c$variable[1:30])
  )

  results_reduced_c <- list()

  for (rc in reduced_configs_c) {
    key_r   <- sprintf("top%d", rc$n)
    label_r <- sprintf("%s — Top %d Predictors", cfg$label, rc$n)

    cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting: %s\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S"), label_r))
    X_sub   <- as.matrix(mydata %>% select(all_of(rc$cols)))
    grp_sub <- group_map %>% filter(variable %in% rc$cols)
    results_reduced_c[[key_r]] <- run_hte(
      outcome_col = cfg$col, label = label_r, direction = cfg$direction,
      ipw_vec = ipw_weights[[key]], mydata = mydata,
      X_mat = X_sub, W_all = W_all, pid_all = pid_all,
      p_cols = rc$cols, grp_map = grp_sub
    )
  }

  all_results_comp[[key]] <- list(
    full  = full_cached[[key]],
    top10 = results_reduced_c$top10,
    top20 = results_reduced_c$top20,
    top30 = results_reduced_c$top30
  )
}

cat("\nAll component reduced models complete.\n")
```

## Combined 5-Outcome Summary (Composite + Components)

```{r summary-combined}
sig_mark <- function(p) dplyr::case_when(p < .001 ~ "***", p < .01 ~ "**", p < .05 ~ "*", TRUE ~ "")

canonical_label <- setNames(
  sapply(component_config, function(x) x$label),
  sapply(component_config, function(x) x$key)
)
n_pred_full <- length(pred_cols)

# Long-form component rows
rows <- list()
for (key in names(all_results_comp)) {
  for (sub in c("full", "top10", "top20", "top30")) {
    r      <- all_results_comp[[key]][[sub]]
    n_pred <- if (sub == "full") n_pred_full else r$n_predictors
    rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- tibble(
      outcome = canonical_label[[key]], key = key, config = sub,
      N = r$N, n_pred = n_pred,
      ATE = r$ATE, ATE_SE = r$ATE_SE, AUTOC = r$AUTOC, AUTOC_SE = r$AUTOC_SE, p = r$p
    )
  }
}
long_df <- bind_rows(rows)

# Composite rows (in-memory objects from Part A)
comp_rows <- list(
  list(r = res_full,                sub = "full"),
  list(r = results_reduced$top10,   sub = "top10"),
  list(r = results_reduced$top20,   sub = "top20"),
  list(r = results_reduced$top30,   sub = "top30")
)
comp_df <- bind_rows(lapply(comp_rows, function(x) {
  tibble(outcome = "Distress Composite", key = "composite", config = x$sub,
         N = x$r$N, n_pred = x$r$n_predictors,
         ATE = x$r$ATE, ATE_SE = x$r$ATE_SE,
         AUTOC = x$r$AUTOC, AUTOC_SE = x$r$AUTOC_SE, p = x$r$p)
}))

combined_df <- bind_rows(comp_df, long_df %>% select(names(comp_df))) %>%
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome,
    levels = c("Distress Composite", "WHO-5 Wellbeing", "PROMIS Anxiety",
               "PROMIS Depression", "PSS-10 Stress"))) %>%
  arrange(outcome, match(config, c("full", "top10", "top20", "top30")))

# Benjamini-Hochberg FDR within each outcome across the three reduced specs
# (Top 10/20/30, one family per outcome). The Full (184-predictor) model is the
# no-selection baseline and keeps its raw p. p_star drives the significance stars
# in the AUTOC table (Table 3): raw p for Full, FDR-adjusted p for the reduced specs.
bh_reduced <- function(p, config) {
  out <- p                                    # Full keeps its raw p
  red <- config != "full"
  out[red] <- p.adjust(p[red], method = "BH")
  out
}
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  mutate(p_star = bh_reduced(p, config),
         p_BH   = ifelse(config == "full", NA_real_, p_star)) %>%
  ungroup()

# ATE matrix (outcome × config)
ate_mat <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(config = factor(config, levels = c("full", "top10", "top20", "top30"),
                         labels = c("Full", "Top 10", "Top 20", "Top 30")),
         ate_str = sprintf("%.3f (%.3f)%s", ATE, ATE_SE,
                           sig_mark(2 * pnorm(-abs(ATE / ATE_SE))))) %>%
  select(Outcome = outcome, N, config, ate_str) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = config, values_from = ate_str)

# AUTOC matrix (outcome × config). Each predictor specification is emitted as a PAIR of
# columns — the AU-TOC estimate (carrying the significance stars) and a separate SE column
# (bare number, no parentheses) — per Ron's "Est (SE) pairs" request. Stars use p_star: raw p
# for Full, FDR-adjusted p (BH within outcome across the three reduced specs) for Top 10/20/30.
# See note in the manuscript Table 2 caption.
autoc_mat <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(config = factor(config, levels = c("full", "top10", "top20", "top30"),
                         labels = c("Full", "Top 10", "Top 20", "Top 30")),
         est = sprintf("%.3f%s", AUTOC, sig_mark(p_star)),
         se  = sprintf("%.3f", AUTOC_SE)) %>%
  select(Outcome = outcome, N, config, est, se) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = config, values_from = c(est, se),
              names_glue = "{config}__{.value}") %>%
  select(Outcome, N,
         `Full`         = `Full__est`,    `Full SE`   = `Full__se`,
         `Top 10`       = `Top 10__est`,  `Top 10 SE` = `Top 10__se`,
         `Top 20`       = `Top 20__est`,  `Top 20 SE` = `Top 20__se`,
         `Top 30`       = `Top 30__est`,  `Top 30 SE` = `Top 30__se`)

knitr::kable(ate_mat,   caption = "Table 2 — ATE (SE) across 5 outcomes × predictor subsets.")
knitr::kable(autoc_mat, caption = "Table 3 — AUTOC (SE) across 5 outcomes × predictor subsets.")
```

## AUTOC FDR Correction Across Reduced Specs (Exploratory)

```{r autoc-fdr}
fdr_df <- combined_df %>%
  filter(config %in% c("top10", "top20", "top30")) %>%
  mutate(config  = factor(config, levels = c("top10", "top20", "top30"),
                          labels = c("Top 10", "Top 20", "Top 30")),
         sig_raw = sig_mark(p),
         sig_BH  = sig_mark(p_BH)) %>%
  arrange(outcome, config) %>%
  select(Outcome = outcome, Spec = config, AUTOC, AUTOC_SE,
         p_raw = p, sig_raw, p_BH, sig_BH)

knitr::kable(fdr_df, digits = 4,
  caption = "AUTOC with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction across the three reduced specs (Top 10/20/30), applied separately within each outcome.")
```

## Variable Importance Distribution Figure (eFigure)

```{r vi-scree}
vi_panels <- list(
  tibble(outcome = "Distress Composite", vi = res_full$vi_df$importance),
  tibble(outcome = "WHO-5 Wellbeing",   vi = vi_results$wellbeing$vi_df$importance),
  tibble(outcome = "PROMIS Anxiety",    vi = vi_results$anxiety$vi_df$importance),
  tibble(outcome = "PROMIS Depression", vi = vi_results$depression$vi_df$importance),
  tibble(outcome = "PSS-10 Stress",     vi = vi_results$stress$vi_df$importance)
)

vi_scree_df <- bind_rows(lapply(vi_panels, function(d) {
  d %>% arrange(desc(vi)) %>% mutate(rank = row_number())
})) %>%
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome,
    levels = c("Distress Composite", "WHO-5 Wellbeing", "PROMIS Anxiety",
               "PROMIS Depression", "PSS-10 Stress")))

p_vi_scree <- ggplot(vi_scree_df, aes(x = rank, y = vi)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(10, 20, 30), colour = "grey70", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(colour = "#01665e", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(colour = "#01665e", size = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ outcome, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Predictor rank (by split-frequency importance)",
       y = "Split-frequency importance",
       title = "Variable importance distribution across full models",
       subtitle = "Dashed lines = ranks 10, 20, 30") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

print(p_vi_scree)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part C — Engagement Analyses

## Load engagement data

```{r eng-load-verify}
# Engagement metrics (week-13 sessions attended and app minutes) are PRE-MERGED
# into the de-identified file. They live on `full_data` (the untouched copy taken
# at read-in); `mydata` was rebuilt into the predictor matrix above and no longer
# carries them. Arm is joined from `mydata` so the coding is identical to the one
# the forests used. The public file carries no exp_group column, so the treatment
# arm is taken from `intervention_group` (control = 0; engagement is
# structurally zero in control).
df_eng <- full_data %>%
  dplyr::select(id, week_13_total_attendance, week_13_app_minutes) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(mydata %>% dplyr::select(id, intervention_group), by = "id") %>%
  dplyr::select(id, intervention_group, week_13_total_attendance, week_13_app_minutes)

stopifnot(nrow(df_eng) == nrow(mydata), !any(is.na(df_eng$intervention_group)))
df_eng$total_session_engagement <- df_eng$week_13_total_attendance
df_eng$total_app_minutes        <- df_eng$week_13_app_minutes

cat("df_eng rows:", nrow(df_eng), "\n")
cat("Intervention-arm rows (intervention_group == 1):",
    sum(df_eng$intervention_group == 1), "\n")
```

## Full-sample CATE assignment (extend top-20 CATEs to non-completers)

Re-fits the 10 top-20 fold causal forests with identical seeds
(sanity-checked to reproduce the cached OOF CATEs exactly), then assigns
each non-completer a CATE = mean of the 10 held-out fold forests, so
engagement can be compared across the full intervention arm.

```{r refit-top20-fold-forests}
Y_raw_t20 <- mydata$distress_factor * -1
keep_t20  <- !is.na(Y_raw_t20) & !is.na(ipw_composite) & ipw_composite > 0

X_t20   <- as.matrix(mydata %>% select(all_of(top20_cols)))[keep_t20, , drop = FALSE]
Y_t20   <- as.numeric(scale(Y_raw_t20[keep_t20]))
W_t20   <- W_all[keep_t20]
wt_t20  <- as.numeric(ipw_composite[keep_t20])
pid_t20 <- pid_all[keep_t20]
N_t20   <- sum(keep_t20)

stopifnot(N_t20 == results_reduced$top20$N,
          identical(pid_t20, results_reduced$top20$tau_df$id))

K <- 10
foldID_t20 <- integer(N_t20)
set.seed(12345)
for (g in 0:1) {
  idx <- which(W_t20 == g)
  foldID_t20[idx] <- sample(rep(1:K, length.out = length(idx)))
}

cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting 10 top-20 fold forests...\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")))
fold_forests_t20 <- vector("list", K)
for (fold in 1:K) {
  train <- which(foldID_t20 != fold)
  set.seed(123456 + fold)
  fold_forests_t20[[fold]] <- causal_forest(
    X_t20[train, , drop = FALSE], Y_t20[train], W_t20[train],
    sample.weights = wt_t20[train], num.trees = 4000, seed = 123456 + fold, num.threads = 1L)
  cat(sprintf("  fold %2d/%d done\n", fold, K))
}
```

```{r refit-oof-sanity}
tau_oof_refit <- rep(NA_real_, N_t20)
for (fold in 1:K) {
  test <- which(foldID_t20 == fold)
  tau_oof_refit[test] <- as.numeric(predict(fold_forests_t20[[fold]],
                                            X_t20[test, , drop = FALSE])$predictions)
}
stopifnot(sum(is.na(tau_oof_refit)) == 0)
diff_max <- max(abs(tau_oof_refit - results_reduced$top20$tau_df$tau))
cat(sprintf("Refit vs. cached OOF tau:  max |diff| = %.2e\n", diff_max))
stopifnot(diff_max < 1e-10)
cat("Sanity check passed — fold forests reproduce cached OOF CATEs exactly.\n")
```

```{r noncompleter-cates}
nc_idx <- which(!keep_t20)
N_nc   <- length(nc_idx)
X_nc   <- as.matrix(mydata[nc_idx, top20_cols, drop = FALSE])
pid_nc <- pid_all[nc_idx]
stopifnot(sum(is.na(X_nc)) == 0)

nc_preds <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = N_nc, ncol = K)
for (fold in 1:K) {
  nc_preds[, fold] <- as.numeric(predict(fold_forests_t20[[fold]], X_nc)$predictions)
}
tau_nc <- rowMeans(nc_preds)
cat(sprintf("Non-completer N: %d  (completer N: %d  ;  total: %d)\n", N_nc, N_t20, N_nc + N_t20))
```

```{r tau-df-full}
tau_df_full <- bind_rows(
  results_reduced$top20$tau_df %>% mutate(completer = TRUE,  source = "OOF (held-out fold)"),
  tibble(id = pid_nc, tau = tau_nc) %>% mutate(completer = FALSE, source = "Mean of 10 fold forests")
)
stopifnot(nrow(tau_df_full) == nrow(mydata), !any(duplicated(tau_df_full$id)))

tau_df_full <- tau_df_full %>%
  mutate(
    benefit_group = case_when(
      tau <  q20_cut ~ "Non-Responders",
      tau >= q80_cut ~ "High Responders",
      TRUE           ~ "Moderate Responders"
    ),
    benefit_group = factor(benefit_group,
      levels = c("Non-Responders", "Moderate Responders", "High Responders"), ordered = TRUE)
  )
cat(sprintf("tau_df_full assembled:  N = %d  (completers = %d, non-completers = %d)\n",
            nrow(tau_df_full), sum(tau_df_full$completer), sum(!tau_df_full$completer)))
```

## Engagement by benefit subgroup (full intervention arm)

```{r eng-fullsample-merge}
int_eng <- df_eng[df_eng$intervention_group == 1, , drop = FALSE]
int_eng$id_chr <- as.character(int_eng$id)

sub_full_df <- tau_df_full %>%
  mutate(id_chr = as.character(id)) %>%
  select(id_chr, benefit_group, completer, tau)

ipw_df <- tibble(id_chr = as.character(mydata$id), ipw = ipw_composite)

merged_full <- int_eng %>%
  inner_join(sub_full_df, by = "id_chr") %>%
  left_join(ipw_df, by = "id_chr")

merged_full$benefit_group <- factor(merged_full$benefit_group,
  levels = c("Non-Responders", "Moderate Responders", "High Responders"))

cat(sprintf("Intervention participants matched to a subgroup: %d (completers = %d, non-completers = %d)\n",
            nrow(merged_full), sum(merged_full$completer), sum(!merged_full$completer)))
```

## Predicted-engagement interaction (does engageability moderate the ITT effect?)

Two 10-fold cross-validated regression forests predict each
participant's engagement from the full baseline predictor set; predicted
engagement is then tested as a moderator in an IPW-weighted linear model
with HC3 robust SEs.

```{r eng-interact-audit}
eng_for_merge <- df_eng %>%
  mutate(id_chr = as.character(id)) %>%
  select(id_chr, total_session_engagement, total_app_minutes)
mydata_id_chr <- as.character(mydata$id)
eng_lookup <- eng_for_merge[match(mydata_id_chr, eng_for_merge$id_chr), , drop = FALSE]
stopifnot(identical(eng_lookup$id_chr, mydata_id_chr))

eng_sess_all <- eng_lookup$total_session_engagement
eng_app_all  <- eng_lookup$total_app_minutes
```

```{r eng-interact-training-sets}
train_mask_sess <- (W_all == 1) & !is.na(eng_sess_all)
train_mask_app  <- (W_all == 1) & !is.na(eng_app_all)
N_train_sess <- sum(train_mask_sess)
N_train_app  <- sum(train_mask_app)
cat(sprintf("Training N — sessions RF: %d  |  app-min RF: %d\n", N_train_sess, N_train_app))
```

```{r eng-rf-sess-fit}
X_tr_sess <- X_all[train_mask_sess, , drop = FALSE]
Y_tr_sess <- as.numeric(eng_sess_all[train_mask_sess])
K <- 10
set.seed(12346)
foldID_sess <- sample(rep(1:K, length.out = N_train_sess))

cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting 10 sessions-RF fold forests...\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")))
fold_forests_sess <- vector("list", K)
for (fold in 1:K) {
  train <- which(foldID_sess != fold)
  set.seed(234567 + fold)
  fold_forests_sess[[fold]] <- regression_forest(
    X_tr_sess[train, , drop = FALSE], Y_tr_sess[train], num.trees = 4000, seed = 234567 + fold, num.threads = 1L)
  cat(sprintf("  sessions fold %2d/%d done\n", fold, K))
}
```

```{r eng-rf-sess-predict}
pred_sess <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(mydata))
for (fold in 1:K) {
  test_in_train <- which(foldID_sess == fold)
  train_rows    <- which(train_mask_sess)[test_in_train]
  pred_sess[train_rows] <- as.numeric(predict(fold_forests_sess[[fold]],
                                              X_all[train_rows, , drop = FALSE])$predictions)
}
nontr_rows <- which(!train_mask_sess)
if (length(nontr_rows) > 0) {
  nt_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(nontr_rows), ncol = K)
  for (fold in 1:K) {
    nt_mat[, fold] <- as.numeric(predict(fold_forests_sess[[fold]],
                                         X_all[nontr_rows, , drop = FALSE])$predictions)
  }
  pred_sess[nontr_rows] <- rowMeans(nt_mat)
}
stopifnot(sum(is.na(pred_sess)) == 0)
r2_sess <- 1 - sum((Y_tr_sess - pred_sess[train_mask_sess])^2) / sum((Y_tr_sess - mean(Y_tr_sess))^2)
cat(sprintf("Sessions RF — CV R^2 (n = %d): %.3f\n", N_train_sess, r2_sess))
```

```{r eng-rf-app-fit}
X_tr_app <- X_all[train_mask_app, , drop = FALSE]
Y_tr_app <- as.numeric(eng_app_all[train_mask_app])
set.seed(12347)
foldID_app <- sample(rep(1:K, length.out = N_train_app))

cat(sprintf("[%s] Fitting 10 app-min RF fold forests...\n", format(Sys.time(), "%H:%M:%S")))
fold_forests_app <- vector("list", K)
for (fold in 1:K) {
  train <- which(foldID_app != fold)
  set.seed(345678 + fold)
  fold_forests_app[[fold]] <- regression_forest(
    X_tr_app[train, , drop = FALSE], Y_tr_app[train], num.trees = 4000, seed = 345678 + fold, num.threads = 1L)
  cat(sprintf("  app-min  fold %2d/%d done\n", fold, K))
}
```

```{r eng-rf-app-predict}
pred_app <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(mydata))
for (fold in 1:K) {
  test_in_train <- which(foldID_app == fold)
  train_rows    <- which(train_mask_app)[test_in_train]
  pred_app[train_rows] <- as.numeric(predict(fold_forests_app[[fold]],
                                             X_all[train_rows, , drop = FALSE])$predictions)
}
nontr_rows <- which(!train_mask_app)
if (length(nontr_rows) > 0) {
  nt_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(nontr_rows), ncol = K)
  for (fold in 1:K) {
    nt_mat[, fold] <- as.numeric(predict(fold_forests_app[[fold]],
                                         X_all[nontr_rows, , drop = FALSE])$predictions)
  }
  pred_app[nontr_rows] <- rowMeans(nt_mat)
}
stopifnot(sum(is.na(pred_app)) == 0)
r2_app <- 1 - sum((Y_tr_app - pred_app[train_mask_app])^2) / sum((Y_tr_app - mean(Y_tr_app))^2)
cat(sprintf("App-min RF — CV R^2 (n = %d): %.3f\n", N_train_app, r2_app))
```

```{r eng-pred-frame}
# Per-participant predicted engagement (used in the interaction table note correlation).
pred_eng_df <- tibble(
  id        = pid_all,
  W         = W_all,
  arm       = if_else(W_all == 1, "Intervention", "Control"),
  pred_sess = pred_sess,
  pred_app  = pred_app
)
```

```{r eng-interact-frame}
ia_df <- mydata %>%
  mutate(id_chr = as.character(id)) %>%
  select(id_chr, distress_factor, intervention_group) %>%
  mutate(
    ipw   = ipw_composite,
    Zsess = as.numeric(scale(pred_sess)),
    Zapp  = as.numeric(scale(pred_app)),
    T     = intervention_group
  )
keep_ia <- !is.na(ia_df$distress_factor) & !is.na(ia_df$ipw) & ia_df$ipw > 0
ia_df   <- ia_df[keep_ia, , drop = FALSE]
cat(sprintf("Interaction analysis N (composite observed, ipw > 0): %d\n", nrow(ia_df)))
```

```{r eng-interact-fits}
fit_sess <- lm(distress_factor ~ T * Zsess, data = ia_df, weights = ipw)
fit_app  <- lm(distress_factor ~ T * Zapp,  data = ia_df, weights = ipw)

if (requireNamespace("sandwich", quietly = TRUE) && requireNamespace("lmtest", quietly = TRUE)) {
  cat("\n--- Sessions × T (HC3) ---\n")
  print(lmtest::coeftest(fit_sess, vcov. = sandwich::vcovHC(fit_sess, type = "HC3")))
  cat("\n--- App minutes × T (HC3) ---\n")
  print(lmtest::coeftest(fit_app,  vcov. = sandwich::vcovHC(fit_app,  type = "HC3")))
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part D — Paper Tables and Figures

## Table 1 — Baseline characteristics by treatment arm

```{r tab1-demographics}
# Table 1. Baseline Characteristics of Participants by Treatment Arm. Note. Values are M (SD)
#   for continuous characteristics and No. (%) for categorical characteristics; percentages are
#   within-arm and may not sum to 100 because of missing data or rounding. Low-frequency health
#   professions are collapsed into "Other" to match the analytic predictor coding. All data were
#   collected at the baseline (preintervention) assessment, before randomization. The two arms are
#   balanced by design (block randomization stratified by state and facility type).
# Built from the raw read (full_data); descriptive only — adds no analytic result.

df1 <- full_data %>%
  mutate(.arm = ifelse(group == "intervention", "Intervention", "Control"))
n_int <- sum(df1$.arm == "Intervention")
n_ctl <- sum(df1$.arm == "Control")

fmt_msd <- function(x, arm) {
  v <- x[df1$.arm == arm]; sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", mean(v, na.rm = TRUE), sd(v, na.rm = TRUE))
}
fmt_np <- function(flag, arm) {
  v <- flag[df1$.arm == arm]; sprintf("%d (%.1f)", sum(v, na.rm = TRUE), 100 * mean(v, na.rm = TRUE))
}
row_cont <- function(label, x)
  data.frame(Characteristic = label, Intervention = fmt_msd(x, "Intervention"),
             Control = fmt_msd(x, "Control"), check.names = FALSE)
row_cat <- function(label, flag)
  data.frame(Characteristic = label, Intervention = fmt_np(flag, "Intervention"),
             Control = fmt_np(flag, "Control"), check.names = FALSE)
hdr <- function(label)
  data.frame(Characteristic = label, Intervention = "", Control = "", check.names = FALSE)
cat_block <- function(header, fct) {
  # Indent category levels with non-breaking spaces (U+00A0): regular spaces are
  # stripped by read_csv(trim_ws = TRUE) in the manuscript and collapsed by Word,
  # so they do not survive to the rendered table. nbsp renders as literal indent.
  indent <- "    "
  rows <- lapply(levels(fct), function(lv) row_cat(paste0(indent, lv), fct == lv & !is.na(fct)))
  do.call(rbind, c(list(hdr(header)), rows))
}

# Sex
g <- tolower(trimws(as.character(df1$gender)))
sex_cat <- factor(dplyr::case_when(
  g %in% c("female", "mujer", "femenino", "f") ~ "Female",
  g %in% c("male", "hombre", "masculino", "m") ~ "Male",
  TRUE ~ "Preferred not to answer"),
  levels = c("Female", "Male", "Preferred not to answer"))

# Profession (collapsed to the 9-category analytic coding)
prof <- as.character(factor(df1$profession, levels = 1:15,
```
