```{r tab1-demographics}
  labels = c("Physicians", "Nurses", "Optometry", "Dentistry", "Biomedical sciences",
             "Psychology", "Health promotion", "Social work", "Chemistry",
             "Radiology technician", "Laboratory technician", "Physical therapy",
             "Nutrition", "Public health", "Other")))
prof[prof %in% c("Optometry", "Biomedical sciences", "Health promotion",
                 "Radiology technician", "Laboratory technician", "Public health")] <- "Other"
prof <- factor(prof, levels = c("Physicians", "Nurses", "Dentistry", "Psychology",
                                "Social work", "Chemistry", "Physical therapy",
                                "Nutrition", "Other"))

# Educational attainment and marital status
ed <- factor(suppressWarnings(as.integer(df1$ed_level_reduced)), levels = c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7),
  labels = c("Secondary", "Technical career", "Bachelor's degree",
             "Graduate: specialty", "Graduate: master's", "Graduate: doctorate"))
mstat <- factor(suppressWarnings(as.integer(df1$marital_status)), levels = 1:5,
  labels = c("Single", "Married", "Cohabiting", "Separated", "Widowed"))

tab1_demographics <- do.call(rbind, list(
  row_cont("Age, y, M (SD)", df1$age),
  cat_block("Sex, No. (%)", sex_cat),
  row_cont("Years working in health care, M (SD)", df1$yrs_work),
  cat_block("Profession, No. (%)", prof),
  cat_block("Educational attainment, No. (%)", ed),
  cat_block("Marital status, No. (%)", mstat)
))
names(tab1_demographics) <- c("Characteristic",
  sprintf("Intervention (n = %d)", n_int), sprintf("Control (n = %d)", n_ctl))

cat("Table 1 built (Intervention n =", n_int, "| Control n =", n_ctl, ")\n")
print(tab1_demographics, row.names = FALSE)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Figure 1 — Observed ATE by predicted-CATE quintile (top-20 model)

```{r fig1-quintile}
# Figure 1. Observed ATE Within Quintiles of Predicted CATE (Top-20 Model).
# Note. ATE = doubly-robust AIPW estimate within each quintile bin of the predicted CATE from
# the top-20 model. Units are SDs of the EFA composite distress score (higher = greater
# benefit). Error bars are 95% CIs. The monotonically increasing gradient confirms that the
# CATE ranking successfully orders participants by observed benefit.
tau20 <- results_reduced$top20$tau_df$tau
stopifnot(length(tau20) == length(eval_forest_top20$Y.hat))

qbreaks <- quantile(tau20, probs = seq(0, 1, length.out = 6), na.rm = TRUE)
qbin    <- cut(tau20, breaks = qbreaks, labels = 1:5, include.lowest = TRUE)

quint_df <- lapply(1:5, function(q) {
  idx <- which(qbin == q)
  a   <- average_treatment_effect(eval_forest_top20, subset = idx)
  tibble(quintile = q, N = length(idx), predicted = mean(tau20[idx]),
         observed = as.numeric(a["estimate"]), se = as.numeric(a["std.err"]))
}) %>% bind_rows()

p_fig1 <- ggplot(quint_df, aes(x = factor(quintile), y = observed)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey60") +
  geom_col(fill = "#01665e", alpha = 0.85, width = 0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = observed - 1.96 * se, ymax = observed + 1.96 * se),
                width = 0.18, colour = "grey25") +
  labs(x = "Predicted-CATE quintile (1 = lowest predicted benefit)",
       y = "Observed ATE (AIPW, within quintile)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10)

print(p_fig1)
```

## Table 2 — ATE matrix / Table 3 — AUTOC matrix

```{r export-tab2-tab3}
# Table 2. Doubly-Robust Average Treatment Effects (ATEs) Across Outcomes and Predictor
#   Specifications. Note. Values are ATE (SE) in SD units of each outcome (distress outcomes
#   sign-reversed so positive = benefit). Full = all 184 predictors; Top 10/20/30 = reduced
#   forests re-fit on predictors ranked by split-frequency variable importance.
# Table 3. AUTOC Statistics Across Outcomes and Predictor Specifications. Note. Values are
#   AUTOC (SE). * p < .05. ** p < .01. *** p < .001. All tests two-sided.
print(ate_mat); print(autoc_mat)
```

## Table 4 — Subgroup ATEs across all five outcomes

```{r export-tab4-subgroup}
# Table 4. Doubly-Robust ATEs (SE) Within Predicted-Benefit Subgroups for All Five Outcomes.
#   Note. Subgroups defined by composite top-20 CATE percentiles: Non-responders = bottom 20%
#   (n = n_lo), Moderate = middle 60% (n = n_mo), High = top 20% (n = n_hi). Values are Est, SE
#   in SD units of each outcome; distress outcomes sign-reversed so positive = benefit. Component
#   Ns may differ slightly due to outcome-specific attrition. EFA Composite High-vs-Non-responder contrast:
#   Δ, SE, z, p (see below).
pid20 <- results_reduced$top20$tau_df$id
benefit_group <- tau_df_20$benefit_group

comp_keys <- list(
  list(key = "wellbeing",  label = "WHO-5 Wellbeing"),
  list(key = "anxiety",    label = "PROMIS Anxiety"),
  list(key = "depression", label = "PROMIS Depression"),
  list(key = "stress",     label = "PSS-10 Stress")
)
comp_ef <- lapply(comp_keys, function(cfg) comp_eval_forests[[cfg$key]])
names(comp_ef) <- sapply(comp_keys, function(x) x$key)

sg_lookup <- data.frame(id = pid20, sg = as.character(benefit_group), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

subgroup_cell <- function(ef, idx) {
  a   <- average_treatment_effect(ef, subset = idx)
  est <- as.numeric(a["estimate"]); se <- as.numeric(a["std.err"])
  p   <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(est / se))   # Wald test of the subgroup ATE against zero
  star <- ifelse(p < .001, "***", ifelse(p < .01, "**", ifelse(p < .05, "*", "")))
  # Estimate (with stars) and SE as separate strings; SE carries no parentheses so it can
  # occupy its own column (Ron's "Est (SE) pairs" request).
  c(est = sprintf("%.3f%s", est, star), se = sprintf("%.3f", se))
}

# Build one table row as Outcome + (Est, SE) pairs for the three subgroups.
subgroup_row <- function(label, nr, mo, hi) {
  data.frame(
    Outcome                  = label,
    `Non-Responders`         = nr["est"], `Non-Responders SE`      = nr["se"],
    `Moderate Responders`    = mo["est"], `Moderate Responders SE` = mo["se"],
    `High Responders`        = hi["est"], `High Responders SE`     = hi["se"],
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, row.names = NULL
  )
}

composite_row <- subgroup_row(
  "Distress Composite",
  subgroup_cell(eval_forest_top20, idx_low),
  subgroup_cell(eval_forest_top20, idx_mod),
  subgroup_cell(eval_forest_top20, idx_high)
)

comp_rows_tbl <- lapply(comp_keys, function(cfg) {
  ef    <- comp_ef[[cfg$key]]$forest
  pid_c <- comp_ef[[cfg$key]]$pid
  matched_sg <- sg_lookup$sg[match(pid_c, sg_lookup$id)]
  subgroup_row(
    cfg$label,
    subgroup_cell(ef, which(matched_sg == "Non-Responders")),
    subgroup_cell(ef, which(matched_sg == "Moderate Responders")),
    subgroup_cell(ef, which(matched_sg == "High Responders"))
  )
})

combined_sg <- bind_rows(composite_row, bind_rows(comp_rows_tbl))

n_lo <- length(idx_low); n_mo <- length(idx_mod); n_hi <- length(idx_high)
cat(sprintf("Table 3 note inputs — subgroup Ns: Non-responders = %d, Moderate = %d, High = %d\n",
            n_lo, n_mo, n_hi))
cat(sprintf("Distress Composite High-vs-Non-responder (Results text, not the note): diff = %.3f (SE = %.3f), z = %.2f, p = %.4g\n",
            diff_est, diff_se, diff_z, diff_p))

print(combined_sg)
```

## Table 5 — Top-20 VI profile by subgroup

```{r export-tab5-profile}
# Table 5. Baseline Predictor Profiles by Benefit Subgroup (Top-20 Model). Note. Bold rows are
#   scale-level summaries: VI is the sum across items; z-score columns show the mean
#   within-variable z-score across items. Item rows show individual VI and within-variable
#   z-scores. VI is from the top-20 model and sums to 1.00 across all 20 items. Z-scores are
#   standardized to the overall completer mean and SD (N = 1,435). PSS-10 item 8 is a
#   self-efficacy item (higher = less stress; reverse of the three helplessness items).
#   Columns: Predictor | VI | Non-Responders | Moderate Responders | High Responders.
print(profile_table_df)
```

## Table 6 — Engagement by predicted-benefit subgroup

```{r export-tab6-eng-subgroup}
# Table 6 (eTable 3). Intervention Engagement by Predicted-Benefit Subgroup (Full Intervention
#   Arm). Note text (computed live below): n, KW chi-square + p for sessions and app minutes,
#   High-vs-Non-responder Holm-adjusted Wilcoxon p, and the right-skew/ANOVA caveat.
eng_desc <- merged_full %>%
  group_by(benefit_group) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    sess_mean = mean(total_session_engagement, na.rm = TRUE),
    sess_sd   = sd(total_session_engagement,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sess_median = median(total_session_engagement, na.rm = TRUE),
    sess_iqr  = IQR(total_session_engagement,  na.rm = TRUE),
    app_mean  = mean(total_app_minutes, na.rm = TRUE),
    app_sd    = sd(total_app_minutes,   na.rm = TRUE),
    app_median = median(total_app_minutes, na.rm = TRUE),
    app_iqr   = IQR(total_app_minutes,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

eng_subgroup_df <- data.frame(
  Subgroup             = as.character(eng_desc$benefit_group),
  N                    = eng_desc$N,
  `Sessions, M (SD)`      = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", eng_desc$sess_mean, eng_desc$sess_sd),
  `Median Sessions (IQR)` = sprintf("%.0f (%.0f)", eng_desc$sess_median, eng_desc$sess_iqr),
  `App min, M (SD)`       = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", eng_desc$app_mean, eng_desc$app_sd),
  `App min, Mdn (IQR)` = sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", eng_desc$app_median, eng_desc$app_iqr),
  check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Live test statistics for the manuscript note.
kw_sess <- kruskal.test(total_session_engagement ~ benefit_group, data = merged_full)
kw_app  <- kruskal.test(total_app_minutes        ~ benefit_group, data = merged_full)
pw_sess <- pairwise.wilcox.test(merged_full$total_session_engagement,
                                merged_full$benefit_group, p.adjust.method = "holm")$p.value
pw_app  <- pairwise.wilcox.test(merged_full$total_app_minutes,
                                merged_full$benefit_group, p.adjust.method = "holm")$p.value
aov_app_p <- summary(aov(total_app_minutes ~ benefit_group, data = merged_full))[[1]]["benefit_group", "Pr(>F)"]
fmt_p_note <- function(p) ifelse(p < .001, "< .001", sprintf("= %.3f", p))
cat(sprintf(paste0("Note inputs — n = %d; sessions KW chi2(2) = %.2f, p %s, High-vs-Non %s; ",
                   "app KW chi2(2) = %.2f, p %s, High-vs-Non %s; app ANOVA p = %.2f\n"),
            nrow(merged_full),
            unname(kw_sess$statistic), fmt_p_note(kw_sess$p.value),
            fmt_p_note(pw_sess["High Responders", "Non-Responders"]),
            unname(kw_app$statistic),  fmt_p_note(kw_app$p.value),
            fmt_p_note(pw_app["High Responders", "Non-Responders"]),
            aov_app_p))

# The omnibus Kruskal-Wallis test (chi-square/p, above) is reported in the manuscript Table A4
# *Note.*; the pairwise High-vs-Non-responder differences are reported in the Results text. So
# the exported CSV stays purely descriptive (no significance rows) — ZJ's call to de-clutter.

print(eng_subgroup_df)
```

## Table 7 — Predicted-engagement interaction

```{r export-tab7-eng-interaction}
# Table 7 (eTable 4). Predicted-Engagement Moderation of the Treatment Effect on Distress.
#   Note. IPW-weighted linear models (distress ~ T + Zhat + T x Zhat) with HC3 robust SEs;
#   n = nrow(ia_df). Entries are b (SE). Distress is the EFA composite in its RAW (non-reversed)
#   metric, so a negative coefficient = lower distress (benefit). Zhat = standardized predicted
#   engagement from a 10-fold CV regression forest (CV R^2 shown). Predicted engagement
#   correlated r (sessions) / r (app minutes) with the top-20 CATE. * p<.05 ** p<.01 *** p<.001.
hc3_coef <- function(fit) {
  if (requireNamespace("sandwich", quietly = TRUE) && requireNamespace("lmtest", quietly = TRUE)) {
    as.data.frame(unclass(lmtest::coeftest(fit, vcov. = sandwich::vcovHC(fit, type = "HC3"))))
  } else as.data.frame(summary(fit)$coefficients)
}
ct_s <- hc3_coef(fit_sess); ct_a <- hc3_coef(fit_app)
star <- function(p) ifelse(p < .001, "***", ifelse(p < .01, "**", ifelse(p < .05, "*", "")))
cell <- function(ct, term) sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)%s", ct[term, 1], ct[term, 2], star(ct[term, 4]))

ce <- pred_eng_df %>%
  mutate(id_chr = as.character(id)) %>%
  inner_join(tau_df_full %>% mutate(id_chr = as.character(id)) %>% select(id_chr, tau), by = "id_chr")
r_sess_tau <- cor(ce$pred_sess, ce$tau)
r_app_tau  <- cor(ce$pred_app,  ce$tau)

eng_ia_df <- data.frame(
  `Predicted engagement`              = c("Sessions attended", "App minutes"),
  `Treatment assignment, b (SE)`      = c(cell(ct_s, "T"),       cell(ct_a, "T")),
  `Pred. engagement, b (SE)`          = c(cell(ct_s, "Zsess"),   cell(ct_a, "Zapp")),
  `Treatment × Pred. engagement, b (SE)` = c(cell(ct_s, "T:Zsess"), cell(ct_a, "T:Zapp")),
  `p (interaction)`                   = c(sprintf("%.3f", ct_s["T:Zsess", 4]), sprintf("%.3f", ct_a["T:Zapp", 4])),
  check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
# CV R2 dropped from the table body (tangential to the focal interaction test);
# its values are reported in the table note instead — see note inputs below.

cat(sprintf("Note inputs — n = %d; r(pred_sess, tau) = %.2f; r(pred_app, tau) = %.2f; CV R2: sessions = %.2f, app = %.2f\n",
            nrow(ia_df), r_sess_tau, r_app_tau, r2_sess, r2_app))

print(eng_ia_df)
```

## eTable 1 — Baseline predictor definitions

```{r export-etable1-predictors}
# eTable 1. Baseline Predictor Definitions. Note. Candidate baseline (preintervention)
#   predictors entered into the full causal forest (total_pred predictors). Continuous
#   predictors comprise individual item responses + demographic/SES indicators (scale totals
#   not entered separately, to avoid collinearity). Categorical predictors are dummy-coded.
#   Counts computed directly from the predictor matrix. Columns: Set | Items | Definition/
#   response format | Variable level.  >>> VERIFY instrument names + response anchors. <<<
pred_defs <- tibble::tribble(
  ~group,                                     ~domain,                        ~measure,                                                            ~ptype,       ~description,
  "WHO-5 items (t1)",                         "Mental health and well-being", "WHO-5 Well-Being Index",                                            "Continuous", "Past-2-week subjective well-being; each item rated 0 (at no time) to 5 (all of the time).",
  "PROMIS Anxiety items (t1)",                "Mental health and well-being", "PROMIS Adult Anxiety short form",                                   "Continuous", "Past-7-day anxiety symptoms; each item rated 1 (never) to 5 (always).",
  "PROMIS Depression items (t1)",             "Mental health and well-being", "PROMIS Adult Depression short form",                                "Continuous", "Past-7-day depressive symptoms; each item rated 1 (never) to 5 (always).",
  "PSS-10 items (t1)",                        "Mental health and well-being", "Perceived Stress Scale (PSS-10)",                                   "Continuous", "Past-month perceived stress; each item rated 0 (never) to 4 (very often).",
  "MBI / Burnout items (t1)",                 "Mental health and well-being", "Maslach Burnout Inventory",                                         "Continuous", "Emotional exhaustion and personal accomplishment items; each rated 0 (never) to 6 (every day).",
  "HMI items (t1)",                           "Well-being skills",            "Healthy Minds Index",                                               "Continuous", "Self-reported well-being skills across awareness, connection, insight, and purpose.",
  "MAIA items (t1)",                          "Well-being skills",            "Multidimensional Assessment of Interoceptive Awareness",            "Continuous", "Body awareness and interoceptive self-regulation; each item rated 0 to 5.",
  "FFMQ Awareness items (t1)",                "Well-being skills",            "Five Facet Mindfulness Questionnaire: Acting with Awareness",       "Continuous", "Attending to present-moment activity; each item rated 1 to 5.",
  "FFMQ Nonreact items (t1)",                 "Well-being skills",            "Five Facet Mindfulness Questionnaire: Nonreactivity",               "Continuous", "Nonreactivity to inner experience; each item rated 1 to 5.",
  "CERQ Reappraisal items (t1)",              "Well-being skills",            "Cognitive Emotion Regulation Questionnaire: Positive Reappraisal",  "Continuous", "Reappraisal-based emotion regulation; each item rated 1 to 5.",
  "IRI EC items (t1)",                        "Well-being skills",            "Interpersonal Reactivity Index: Empathic Concern",                  "Continuous", "Other-oriented feelings of sympathy and concern; each item rated 1 to 5.",
  "Compassion items (t1)",                    "Well-being skills",            "Compassion scale",                                                  "Continuous", "Dispositional compassion toward others.",
  "Meaning in Life items (t1)",               "Well-being skills",            "Meaning in Life Questionnaire",                                     "Continuous", "Presence of meaning in life; each item rated 1 to 7.",
  "Gratitude items (t1)",                     "Well-being skills",            "Gratitude Questionnaire-6 (GQ-6)",                                  "Continuous", "Dispositional gratitude; each item rated 1 to 7.",
  "Character strengths / GACS-24 items (t1)", "Well-being skills",            "Global Assessment of Character Strengths (GACS-24)",                "Continuous", "Self-rated character strengths.",
  "Demographics / SES",                       "Demographic and occupational", "Demographic and socioeconomic indicators",                          "Continuous", "Age, years working in health care, and household composition (people, rooms, bathrooms, internet access, vehicles).",
  "Gender",                                   "Demographic and occupational", "Gender",                                                            "Categorical",  "Male versus not male.",
  "State (dummy)",                            "Demographic and occupational", "State of residence",                                                "Categorical",  "Mexican state (one indicator per state).",
  "Profession (dummy)",                       "Demographic and occupational", "Health profession",                                                 "Categorical",  "Profession category (e.g., medicine, nursing, psychology).",
  "Job position (dummy)",                     "Demographic and occupational", "Job position",                                                      "Categorical",  "Occupational role category.",
  "Education (dummy)",                         "Demographic and occupational", "Education level",                                                   "Categorical",  "Highest completed education level.",
  "Marital status (dummy)",                   "Demographic and occupational", "Marital status",                                                    "Categorical",  "Marital status category.",
  "Training / clinical history",              "Demographic and occupational", "Prior training history",                                            "Continuous & categorical", "Prior socioemotional/contemplative training (yes/no) and cumulative training hours."
)

counts <- group_map %>% dplyr::count(group, name = "n_pred")
etable1_df <- pred_defs %>% dplyr::left_join(counts, by = "group")

missing_def <- setdiff(unique(group_map$group), pred_defs$group)
if (length(missing_def) > 0) stop("group_map groups with no definition row: ", paste(missing_def, collapse = ", "))
if (any(is.na(etable1_df$n_pred))) warning("Definition rows with no matching predictors.")
total_pred <- sum(etable1_df$n_pred, na.rm = TRUE)
stopifnot(total_pred == length(pred_cols))
if (total_pred != 184L) warning(sprintf("Predictor total is %d, not 184.", total_pred))
cat(sprintf("eTable 1 predictor total: %d (length(pred_cols) = %d).\n", total_pred, length(pred_cols)))

domain_levels <- c("Mental health and well-being", "Well-being skills", "Demographic and occupational")
etable1_display <- etable1_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(domain = factor(domain, levels = domain_levels)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(domain, dplyr::desc(n_pred)) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    Set                            = measure,
    Items                          = n_pred,
    `Definition / response format` = description,
    `Variable level`               = ptype
  )

print(etable1_display)
```

## eTable 2 — Outcome correlation matrix

```{r export-etable2-outcome-cors}
# eTable 2. Pairwise Correlations Among the Five 37-Week Outcomes. Note. Pairwise Pearson
#   correlations (pairwise-complete observations; N range n_min–n_max). Distress scales
#   correlate negatively with WHO-5 well-being and positively with one another. The EFA
#   Distress Composite is the single-factor score used as the primary CATE outcome.
#   * p<.05 ** p<.01 *** p<.001.
outcome_specs <- tibble::tribble(
  ~col,            ~label,
  "t8_wellbeing",  "WHO-5 Well-Being",
  "t8_anxiety",    "PROMIS Anxiety",
  "t8_depression", "PROMIS Depression",
  "t8_stress",     "PSS-10 Stress"
)
missing_cols <- setdiff(outcome_specs$col, names(full_data))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("eTable 2: outcome columns not found in full_data: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

out_df <- full_data %>%
  dplyr::select(id, dplyr::all_of(outcome_specs$col)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(distress_factor_raw %>% dplyr::select(id, distress_factor), by = "id")

cor_cols   <- c("distress_factor", outcome_specs$col)
cor_labels <- c("Distress Composite", outcome_specs$label)
M          <- as.matrix(out_df[, cor_cols]); colnames(M) <- cor_labels

cmat <- cor(M, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")

k <- ncol(M); pmat <- matrix(NA_real_, k, k); nmat <- matrix(NA_integer_, k, k)
for (i in seq_len(k)) for (j in seq_len(k)) {
  if (i == j) next
  ok <- stats::complete.cases(M[, i], M[, j])
  nmat[i, j] <- sum(ok); pmat[i, j] <- stats::cor.test(M[ok, i], M[ok, j])$p.value
}
ns <- nmat[lower.tri(nmat)]; n_min <- min(ns); n_max <- max(ns)

star2 <- function(p) ifelse(is.na(p), "", ifelse(p < .001, "***", ifelse(p < .01, "**", ifelse(p < .05, "*", ""))))
stars <- matrix(star2(pmat), k, k)

disp <- formatC(round(cmat, 2), format = "f", digits = 2)
disp <- sub("^(-?)0\\.", "\\1.", disp)
disp <- matrix(paste0(disp, as.vector(stars)), k, k)
disp[upper.tri(disp)] <- ""; diag(disp) <- "—"
cor_df <- data.frame(Outcome = cor_labels, disp, check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
colnames(cor_df) <- c("Outcome", cor_labels); rownames(cor_df) <- NULL

cat(sprintf("eTable 2 pairwise-complete N range: %s–%s\n",
            format(n_min, big.mark = ","), format(n_max, big.mark = ",")))

print(cor_df)
```

## IPW diagnostics

```{r export-ipw-diagnostics}
# IPW response-model diagnostics for eMethods: per-arm cross-validated AUC (95% CI) and
# normalized-weight ranges among completers.
print(ipw_diagnostics)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Reproduction Complete

```{r repro-complete}
cat("All paper tables and figures reproduced inline above.\n")
cat("Pipeline integrity check (composite N / ATE / AUTOC / SE) passed earlier in the run.\n")
sessionInfo()
```
